name: Ming-Omni
io: I, T, A, V → I, T, A
tokenizer: Vision encoder: Qwen2.5 ViT; Audio encoder: Whisper; Image generation: Ming-Lite-Uni diffusion model; Audio decoder: custom TTS decoder
task: Multimodal Understanding (VQA, OCR, GUI, Video QA, Audio QA), Image Generation, Speech Generation, Image Grounding
dataset:
Training: Proprietary multimodal datasets (image-text, video-text, audio-text)
Benchmarks: OpenCompass (image), RefCOCO/+/g (grounding), ChartQA, DocVQA, OCRBenchV2, TextVQA, ScreenSpot, MVBench, VideoMME, MMAU, VoiceBench, Seed-TTS-Eval, GenEval, DPG-Bench
metric: Accuracy, CER, WER, FID, SEED-TTS-Eval score, mIoU
highlight: Ming-Omni is reportedly the first open-source model matching GPT-4o in modality coverage, processing images, text, audio, and video while generating text, audio, and images through a unified MoE-based architecture with modality-specific routing.

name: MMaDA
tokenizer: Image tokenizer: MAGVIT-v2 (discrete VQ tokens); Text tokenizer: BPE
task: Text-to-Image Generation, Visual Question Answering, Image Captioning, Mathematical Reasoning, Logical Reasoning
dataset:
Training: RefinedWeb, LAION-Aesthetics, JourneyDB (pretraining); Alpaca, LLaVA-1.5 (instruction tuning); GSM8K, MATH, OpenThoughts (CoT fine-tuning); GSM8K, GeoQA, CLEVR (RL)
Benchmarks: MMLU, ARC-C, TruthfulQA, VQAv2, MMMU, POPE, GenEval, WISE, ImageReward
metric: Accuracy (reasoning), VQA Accuracy, CLIP Score, ImageReward, GenEval Score, WISE Cultural Score, FID
highlight: MMaDA is a unified multimodal diffusion model handling both language reasoning and image generation in a single framework, trained with a novel UniGRPO reinforcement learning algorithm for multi-modal alignment.

name: BAGEL
tokenizer: Visual understanding: SigLIP2 ViT encoder; Visual generation: FLUX VAE encoder/decoder
task: Text-to-Image Generation, Text-to-Video Generation, Image Editing, Visual Question Answering, Chart/Diagram Reasoning, Spatial Reasoning
dataset:
Training: High-quality general text; web image-text pairs (CLIP-filtered); artistic/surreal captions; video frames from Koala36M, MVImgNet2.0; interleaved web documents
Benchmarks: MME, MMBench (understanding); GenEval, WISE (generation); GEdit-Bench, IntelligentBench (editing)
metric: IntelligentBench Score, GenEval Score, WISE Score, GEdit-Bench Score, FID, CLIP Score
highlight: BAGEL employs a Mixture-of-Transformers (MoT) architecture with separate expert heads for understanding and generation, achieving strong performance on intelligent editing tasks requiring world knowledge and complex reasoning.

name: VITA-Audio
tokenizer: Audio encoder and decoder: CosyVoice codec
task: Spoken Question Answering, Automatic Speech Recognition, Text-to-Speech Synthesis
dataset:
Training: Proprietary audio instruction-tuning datasets
Benchmarks: Llama Question, TriviaQA, Web Question (SQA); SEED-TTS, LibriTTS (TTS); WenetSpeech, AISHELL, LibriSpeech, FLEURS (ASR)
metric: Accuracy (SQA), WER (ASR), CER (TTS), Speaker Similarity, Latency (tokens/sec)
ps: Proposes Multiple Cross-modal Token Prediction (MCTP) module to generate audio tokens from text embeddings and LLM hidden states simultaneously.
highlight: VITA-Audio introduces Multiple Cross-modal Token Prediction (MCTP), which generates audio tokens conditioned on both text embeddings and LLM hidden states, enabling end-to-end speech dialogue with reduced first-token latency.

name: Kimi-Audio
tokenizer: Audio tokenizer: 12.5 Hz discrete audio tokenizer; Audio detokenizer: chunk-wise streaming flow-matching model
backbone: Pre-trained LLM (continually pre-trained on audio data)
task: Automatic Speech Recognition, Speech Translation, Audio Understanding, Audio Question Answering, Speech Conversation
dataset:
Training: 13M+ hours of audio data (speech, sound, music) + large text corpus
Benchmarks: LibriSpeech, FLEURS, AISHELL-1/2, WenetSpeech (ASR); MMAU, ClothoAQA, VocalSound, Nonspeech7k, MELD, TUT2017, CochlScene (audio understanding); OpenAudioBench, VoiceBench (chat)
metric: WER, CER (ASR); Accuracy (audio understanding); Human Ratings (conversation)
highlight: Kimi-Audio is a fully open-source universal audio foundation model trained on over 13 million hours of audio data, achieving state-of-the-art results across speech recognition, audio understanding, and conversational tasks.

name: MetaQueries
tokenizer: Vision encoder: CLIP ViT; Image decoder: VAE decoder
backbone: Frozen MLLM (LLaVA/Qwen2.5 series) + Stable Diffusion v1.5 / Sana diffusion model
task: Text-to-Image Generation, Visual Question Answering, Image Editing, Subject-Driven Generation, Knowledge-Augmented Image Generation
dataset:
Training: 25M public image-text pairs; 2.4M instruction-tuning samples
Benchmarks: MME, MMBench, MMMU (understanding); COCO FID, MJHQ-30K FID, GenEval, DPG-Bench (generation); WISE, CommonsenseT2I (knowledge reasoning); DreamBench (subject generation)
metric: FID (COCO, MJHQ-30K), GenEval Score, DPG-Bench Score, DINO/CLIP-I/CLIP-T (subject), WiScore (knowledge), Accuracy (VQA)
highlight: MetaQueries bridges frozen multimodal LLMs with diffusion models via a lightweight learnable query module, enabling knowledge-augmented image generation (e.g., cultural landmarks, commonsense concepts) without fine-tuning the MLLM backbone.

name: Qwen2.5-Omni
io: I, T, A, V → T, S
tokenizer: Text: Qwen2.5 byte-level BPE; Vision: ViT-based encoder (~675M params, from Qwen2.5-VL); Audio input: Mel-spectrogram encoder (from Qwen2-Audio); Speech output: Flow-Matching DiT + BigVGAN waveform decoder
backbone: Thinker-Talker dual-component architecture (Thinker: 7B LLM for text; Talker: streaming speech token generator)
task: Automatic Speech Recognition, Speech Translation, Speech Emotion Recognition, Music Classification, Audio Reasoning, Visual Question Answering, OCR, Video Understanding, Zero-Shot TTS, Streaming Speech Dialogue
dataset:
Training: 800B image-text tokens, 300B audio-text tokens, 100B video-audio tokens, large text corpus; sequence length extended to 32K
Benchmarks: MMLU-Pro, GSM8K, MATH, HumanEval (text); LibriSpeech, CoVoST2, MELD, MMAU (audio); MMMU, MathVista, MMBench, OCRBench (image); Video-MME, MVBench, EgoSchema (video); SEED-TTS (speech generation); VoiceBench (voice interaction)
metric: Accuracy, Pass@1 (text); WER, BLEU, F1 (audio); Accuracy, ANLS, mAP (visual); WER, SIM, NMOS (speech generation)
ps: Thinker-Talker architecture: Thinker handles text generation while Talker generates streaming speech tokens from Thinker's hidden representations. Inspired by Mini-Omni.
highlight: Qwen2.5-Omni adopts a novel Thinker-Talker architecture enabling real-time streaming speech output, where the Talker module directly receives hidden states from the Thinker LLM to generate speech tokens without a separate TTS pipeline.

name: Baichuan-Audio
tokenizer: Audio encoder: Whisper + multi-codebook RVQ discretization (12.5 Hz); Audio decoder: Pre-Net + flow-matching conditional decoder
task: Spoken Question Answering, Automatic Speech Recognition, Speech Translation, Text-to-Speech Synthesis, Audio Commonsense Reasoning
dataset:
Training: Proprietary audio and text datasets (publicly available upon release)
Benchmarks: sStoryCloze, zh-sStoryCloze (sentence continuation); sCMMLU (commonsense); CoVoST-2 zh↔en (translation); SEED-TTS (TTS); Llama Questions, TriviaQA, Web Questions, AlpacaEval (SQA)
metric: Accuracy (reasoning), BLEU (translation), CER/WER (ASR/TTS), Speaker Similarity
highlight: An end-to-end audio LLM with a two-stage pre-training strategy that maintains language understanding while enhancing audio modeling, supporting seamless bidirectional speech interaction with text-guided aligned speech generation.

name: Qwen2.5-VL
tokenizer: Vision: native dynamic-resolution ViT with patch splitting and feature compression; Position encoding: 2D RoPE (images), 3D MRoPE (video); Text: Qwen2.5 tokenizer
task: Visual Question Answering, Document Parsing, OCR, Object Grounding, Video Understanding, GUI Agent Operation, Multi-step Decision Making
dataset:
Training: 4.1T tokens (interleaved image-text, OCR, video, documents); 2M SFT samples; 1M synthetic charts, 6M real-world tables, long-video captions
Benchmarks: MMMU, MathVista, OCRBench_v2, Charades-STA, ScreenSpot Pro, CountBench, MMBench, DocVQA
metric: Accuracy, F1 Score, ANLS, ROUGE-L, mAP, mIoU, Success Rate (agent)
highlight: Qwen2.5-VL uses a dynamic-resolution native ViT with Window Attention and 3D MRoPE for temporal video encoding, achieving leading performance on document understanding, fine-grained grounding, long-video comprehension, and GUI agent tasks.

name: HermesFlow
tokenizer: Discrete visual tokens (Show-o tokenizer)
task: Text-to-Image Generation, Visual Question Answering, Image Captioning, Object Hallucination Detection
dataset:
Training: 5,000 JourneyDB image-caption pairs; TIFA-generated QA pairs + JourneyDB VQA data
Benchmarks: POPE, MME, Flickr30k, VQAv2, GQA, MMMU (understanding); GenEval, DPG-Bench, MSCOCO-30K FID/CLIP-Score (generation)
metric: Accuracy, BERT Similarity, FID, CLIP Score, GenEval Score, DPG-Bench Score
highlight: HermesFlow introduces Pair-DPO, a self-play iterative alignment method using homologous data pairs to simultaneously close the understanding-generation gap in unified multimodal models.

name: Step-Audio
tokenizer: Proprietary audio tokenizer (Step-Audio-Tokenizer)
task: Automatic Speech Recognition, Text-to-Speech Synthesis, Audio Question Answering, Audio Instruction Following
dataset:
Training: Large-scale proprietary speech and audio data
Benchmarks: AISHELL-1/2, WenetSpeech, LibriSpeech (ASR); SEED-TTS (TTS); StepEval-Audio-360 (audio chat); Proprietary audio instruction-following benchmark
metric: CER (Chinese ASR/TTS), WER (English ASR/TTS), Speaker Similarity, Factuality Score, Relevance Score, Chat Score (GPT-4o-assessed), Mean Opinion Score (MOS)
ps: Despite end-to-end dialogue capability, the model uses LLM+TTS for practical advantages (voice control, richer training data). End-to-end nature is defined by the LLM's ability to perceive audio features directly.
highlight: Step-Audio is the first production-ready open-source audio LLM at 130B scale, supporting controllable emotion/rhythm/dialect in speech generation and demonstrating strong performance across ASR, TTS, and audio conversational tasks.

name: Janus-Pro
tokenizer: Understanding: SigLIP-Large-Patch16-384 (continuous features); Generation: VQ tokenizer (discrete tokens)
task: Visual Question Answering, Visual Reasoning, Document/Chart Understanding, Text-to-Image Generation
dataset:
Training: 90M+ samples (YFCC, Docmatix, table/chart/doc data) for understanding; 72M synthetic aesthetic samples (Midjourney prompts) for generation; 1:1 real-to-synthetic ratio
Benchmarks: POPE, MME, GQA, MMBench, SEED, MM-Vet, MMMU (understanding); GenEval, DPG-Bench (generation)
metric: Accuracy (VQA), GenEval Score (compositional generation), DPG-Bench Score (dense prompt following)
highlight: Janus-Pro improves upon Janus by decoupling visual encoding for understanding and generation with an optimized training strategy, expanded data, and larger model scale, achieving state-of-the-art text-to-image generation among unified models.

name: PackDiT
tokenizer: VAE (motion sequence encoder/decoder)
task: Text-to-Motion Generation, Motion-to-Text Generation, Joint Motion-Text Generation, Unconditional Motion Generation
dataset:
Training: HumanML3D
Benchmarks: HumanML3D test split
metric: FID, DIV (diversity), R-Precision, BLEU, CIDEr
ps: Mo = Motion (human body motion sequences, not music). PackDiT packs both motion and text diffusion into a single DiT model with dual-task training.
highlight: PackDiT trains a single Diffusion Transformer jointly for bidirectional motion-text generation, enabling both text-to-motion and motion-to-text tasks without separate model heads, using a packed multi-task diffusion objective.

name: Baichuan-Omni-1.5
io: I, V, A, T → T, S
tokenizer: Vision: NaViT-based encoder (from Qwen2-VL); Audio: Baichuan-Audio-Tokenizer (semantic + acoustic dual-codebook RVQ)
task: Visual Question Answering, Video Question Answering, Audio Question Answering, OCR, Chart/Graph Reasoning, Medical Image Diagnosis, Text-to-Speech Synthesis
dataset:
Training: ~500B high-quality multimodal tokens; LAION-5B, DenseFusion-1M, Synthdog, InternVL-SA-1B-Caption (image); ShareGPT4Video, WebVid, ActivityNet-QA (video); ASR, TTS, speech translation, interleaved audio-text data
Benchmarks: MMBench, SEEDBench, RealWorldQA, HallusionBench (general); OpenAudioBench, VoiceBench (audio); OpenMM-Medical, GMAI-MMBench (medical); OmniBench (omni); MMLU, CMMLU (language)
metric: Accuracy, Score (task-specific)
highlight: Baichuan-Omni-1.5 introduces a dual-codebook audio tokenizer capturing both semantic and acoustic information, combined with a multi-stage training strategy for progressive multimodal alignment, achieving GPT-4o-mini-level omni-modal performance with strong medical imaging capabilities.

name: VARGPT
tokenizer: Visual encoder: CLIP ViT-L/14 (understanding); Visual decoder: VAE decoder (generation)
task: Visual Question Answering, Visual Instruction Following, Text-to-Image Generation
dataset:
Training: ImageNet (pretraining); Proprietary Imagenet-Instruct-based SFT datasets
Benchmarks: GQA, TextVQA, VQAv2, SciQA-img, OKVQA, VizWizQA (instruction following); MMBench, SEED-Bench, MMMU, POPE, MME (visual QA); MJHQ-30K (generation)
metric: VQA Accuracy, CLIP Score, FID
highlight: VARGPT integrates visual autoregressive generation (VAR) into a MLLM framework, enabling unified visual understanding and next-scale prediction-based image generation within a single autoregressive model.

name: MiniCPM-o 2.6
io: I, V, T, S → T, S
tokenizer: Vision: SigLIP ViT + MobileSAM; Speech: CosyVoice-based encoder/decoder
task: Visual Question Answering, Chart/Diagram Reasoning, Document Parsing, Image Captioning, Open-Vocabulary Object Detection, Speech Interaction, Real-Time Live Streaming Analysis
dataset:
Training: TextOCR (OCR data); diverse multimodal instruction data
Benchmarks: MMBench, MMMU, MathVista (general); DocVQA, TextVQA, ChartQA (OCR/document); ODinW (detection); VoiceBench (speech)
metric: Accuracy, ANLS, mAP, MMBench Score, MMStar Score, Mobile RAM usage
highlight: MiniCPM-o 2.6 is a GPT-4o-level omni-modal LLM optimized for on-device deployment (as low as 2.9B on mobile), supporting real-time multimodal live streaming with vision, speech, and text understanding/generation.

name: D-DiT
tokenizer: Image: VAE (SD3); Text: T5 encoder
task: Text-to-Image Generation, Image-to-Text Generation (VQA), Image-to-Text Infilling
dataset:
Training: Datacomp-1B, ShareGPT4V, OpenImages, LAION-1024, Midjourney, LLaVA-Pretrain-558K, LLaVA-v1.5-665K, TextVQA, VizWiz, VQAv2, OKVQA, GQA, OpenWebText
Benchmarks: GenEval, MS-COCO, T2I-CompBench, MJHQ-30K, POPE, MME
metric: FID, CLIP Score, VQA Accuracy, GenEval Score, CIDEr, Color/Shape/Texture alignment
highlight: D-DiT introduces a dual-branch diffusion transformer that jointly trains image generation (flow matching) and text generation (absorbing-state discrete diffusion) with a single unified loss, leveraging bidirectional attention for cross-modal token interaction.

name: MetaMorph
io: I, V, T → I, V, T
tokenizer: SigLIP (visual encoder for understanding); continuous visual features for generation
task: Image Question Answering, Video Question Answering, Text-to-Image Generation, Video Frame Prediction, Image-to-Image Translation, Visual Chain-of-Thought Reasoning
dataset:
Training: Cambrian-7M (ImageQA); VideoStar, ShareVideo (VideoQA); MetaCLIP (generation); Something-Something-V2, HowTo100M (video prediction); Visualization-of-Thought, VStar (visual reasoning); InstructPix2Pix, Aurora (I2I translation)
Benchmarks: MMBench, SEED, VStar, MMVP, MMMU, ChartQA, TextVQA, ScienceQA, RealWorldQA (ImageQA); MV-Bench (VideoQA)
metric: FID, CLIP Score, QA Accuracy
highlight: MetaMorph is a unified multimodal LLM using visual tokens as both input and output, trained jointly across diverse multimodal tasks including generation, prediction, and visual chain-of-thought, showing emergent capabilities from large-scale multi-task learning.

name: ILLUME
tokenizer: UNIT tokenizer (unified image tokenizer based on VQ-VAE for both understanding and generation)
task: Multimodal Understanding, Text-to-Image Generation, Image Editing
dataset:
Training: LLaVA-Pretrain (understanding)
Benchmarks: POPE, MMBench, SEED, MME-P, MM-Vet, MMMU, AI2D, VQA-text, ChartQA, DocVQA, InfoVQA, OCRBench (understanding); MJHQ-30K, GenAI-Bench, GenEval (generation); Emu Edit (editing)
metric: Accuracy (understanding), FID, CLIP-I, CLIP-T, DINO (generation/editing)
highlight: ILLUME introduces a unified image tokenizer (UNIT) that serves both multimodal understanding and generation tasks within a single autoregressive LLM, enabling coherent image editing alongside perception and generation without separate codebooks.

name: InternVL 2.5
tokenizer: InternViT (vision encoder with dynamic resolution)
task: Visual Question Answering, Mathematical Reasoning, OCR, Chart/Document Understanding, Multi-Image Reasoning, Visual Grounding, Video Understanding, Multilingual Multimodal Understanding
dataset:
Training: Proprietary large-scale multimodal dataset (filtered from diverse public sources)
Benchmarks: MMMU, MMMU-Pro, MathVista, MathVerse, OlympiadBench (reasoning); AI2D, ChartQA, TextVQA, DocVQA, OCRBench (OCR/document); MME, MMBench, MMStar, POPE, HallusionBench (general); RefCOCO/+/g (grounding); Video-MME, MVBench, MLVU (video); MMLU, GSM8K, MATH, HumanEval (language)
metric: Accuracy, ANLS, Jaccard, mAP, Win Rate, CIDEr
highlight: InternVL 2.5 scales vision-language models from 1B to 78B with dynamic-resolution InternViT and flexible LLM backbones, achieving leading performance on multimodal reasoning, OCR, video understanding, and multilingual evaluation benchmarks.

name: Liquid
tokenizer: VQGAN (discrete image tokens)
task: Text-to-Image Generation, Visual Question Answering, Language Modeling
dataset:
Training: DCLM, SlimPajama, StarCoderData (text); LMSYS, Minigeni (image-text pairs); JourneyDB, internal MidJourney-style synthetic data (instruction tuning)
Benchmarks: GenAI-Bench, MJHQ-30K, WISE (generation); VQAv2, GQA, TextVQA, POPE, MME (understanding); HellaSwag, WinoGrande, ARC-Easy/Challenge, BoolQ, MMLU (language)
metric: VQA Score, FID, WiScore, Accuracy, Hallucination Rate
highlight: Liquid trains a single decoder-only transformer jointly on text, image understanding, and image generation tasks without separate encoder/decoder modules, demonstrating that scaling a unified autoregressive model improves both visual generation quality and language capabilities simultaneously.

name: GPT4Point
tokenizer: Point-QFormer (BLIP-2-style Q-Former aligning point cloud and text); CLIP text encoder; Point-E CLIP tokenizer for generation
backbone: Point-BERT (point encoder); OPT-2.7B / OPT-6.7B / FLAN-T5-XL (LLM); Point-E (3D generation)
task: 3D Object Recognition (zero-shot classification, point-text retrieval), 3D Point Cloud Captioning, 3D Point Cloud Question Answering, 3D Point Cloud Generation
dataset:
Training: Objaverse, Objaverse-XL (annotated via multi-stage Pyramid-XL captioning pipeline with VLM)
Benchmarks: ModelNet40 (classification); Objaverse-LVIS (retrieval/captioning); Cap3D (generation comparison)
metric: Acc@1 (classification), R@1/5/10 (retrieval), BLEU-1/4, METEOR, ROUGE-L, CIDEr (captioning), FID, CLIP Score, User Study Score (generation)
highlight: GPT4Point proposes a multi-stage Pyramid-XL captioning pipeline to automatically annotate large-scale 3D datasets (Objaverse-XL), and trains a unified model for 3D point cloud understanding (captioning, QA) and generation within a single framework.

name: GLM-4-Voice
tokenizer: Speech encoder: Whisper-large-v3 with pooling + VQ layer; Speech decoder: CosyVoice-based (speech token encoder + conditional flow matching + HiFi-GAN vocoder)
task: Spoken Question Answering, Speech Language Modeling, Speech Dialogue, Emotionally/Style-Controllable TTS
dataset:
Training: Large-scale proprietary speech-text paired data
Benchmarks: spoken StoryCloze, spoken Topic-StoryCloze (speech LM); Web Questions, Llama Questions, TriviaQA (spoken QA); proprietary General QA and Knowledge sets (chat)
metric: Accuracy (spoken QA), ChatGPT Score (GPT-4o-assessed, chat quality), UTMOS (speech quality), WER (speech-text alignment)
highlight: GLM-4-Voice supports bilingual (Chinese/English) real-time voice conversation with controllable vocal attributes including emotion, intonation, speech rate, and dialect, using a Whisper-based encoder and CosyVoice-based streaming speech decoder integrated with GLM-4.

name: KE-Omni
tokenizer: Speech encoder: Whisper-large-v3; Speech decoder: duration predictor + speech unit generator + unit-based vocoder
task: Speech-to-Text Instruction Following, Automatic Speech Recognition, Text-to-Speech Synthesis
dataset:
Training: Proprietary speech instruction data
Benchmarks: Proprietary in-house evaluation set, VoiceBench
metric: S2TIF Score (GPT-4-assessed instruction following), WER/CER (speech-text alignment), UTMOS (speech quality)
highlight: KE-Omni integrates knowledge enhancement into an omni speech model, enabling LLaMA-3.1 to handle speech instruction following while maintaining knowledge-rich spoken dialogue with both text and speech input/output.

name: OmniFlow
io: I, T, A → I, T, A
tokenizer: VAE (image/audio continuous latents); Text: T5 encoder
task: Text-to-Image Generation, Text-to-Audio Generation, Audio-to-Image Generation, Image-to-Audio Generation, Any-to-Any Multimodal Generation
dataset:
Training: COYO-700M, LAION-Aesthetic-3M, CC12M, LAION-COCO (image-text); AudioSet, AudioCaps, WavCaps, VGGSound, SoundNet (audio); T2I-2M (image-text)
Benchmarks: MSCOCO-30K, GenEval (image generation); proprietary audio evaluation
metric: FID (image quality), CLIP Score (text-image alignment), FAD (audio quality), CLAP Score (text-audio alignment), CIDEr (captioning)
highlight: OmniFlow generalizes Rectified Flow to any-modal generation, introducing path encoding and multi-modal guidance mechanisms that allow independent control of each input modality's influence, trained efficiently with only 30M images (vs. 400M for CoDi).

name: JetFormer
tokenizer: Continuous image tokens via learned encoder/decoder (no VQ discretization)
task: Class-Conditional Image Generation, Text-to-Image Generation, Image Captioning, Visual Question Answering
dataset:
Training: ImageNet-1K (class-conditional), WebLI (image-text)
Benchmarks: ImageNet-1K (generation); COCO (captioning); VQAv2 (VQA)
metric: FID, Precision, Recall, NLL, CIDEr, VQAv2 Accuracy
highlight: JetFormer directly models continuous image tokens with an autoregressive transformer without vector quantization, enabling high-fidelity image generation and understanding within a single model using a unified continuous-token objective.

name: Orthus
task: Multimodal Understanding, Text-to-Image Generation, Mixed-Modality Generation (interleaved image-text)
dataset:
Training: LLaVA-v1.5-665K (understanding); JourneyDB, LAION-COCO-aesthetic recaptioned from ShareGPT-4V (generation); StoryStream (narrative generation)
Benchmarks: POPE, MME-P, VQAv2, GQA, MMMU (understanding); GenEval, HPSv2 (generation)
metric: Accuracy (VQA), GenEval Score, HPSv2 Score
highlight: Orthus combines autoregressive LLM understanding with diffusion-based image generation through a decoupled two-head architecture, enabling coherent mixed-modality narrative generation (interleaved text and images) without compromising either understanding or generation quality.

name: UniPose
io: Po, I, T → Po, I, T
tokenizer: VQ-VAE (pose tokens); CLIP ViT + pose-specific ViT (image encoder)
task: Pose-to-Text Description, Image Pose Difference Captioning, Text-to-Pose Generation, Pose Estimation, Pose Editing
dataset:
Training: AMASS, MOYO (pose tokenizer); PoseScript, PoseFix, Human3.6M, MPI-INF-3DHP, COCO, MPII, ImageScript, ImageDiff (UniPose training)
Benchmarks: 3DPW, Human3.6M
metric: R-Precision Top-1/2/3, BLEU-4, ROUGE-L, METEOR (pose comprehension); MPJPE, PA-MPJPE, RT2P, RP2T (pose generation); FID (pose editing)
ps: Po = Pose (human body pose sequences in 3D)
highlight: UniPose is a unified framework for human pose comprehension, generation, and editing within a single LLM, using VQ-VAE-based pose tokenization and supporting bidirectional pose-language tasks including cross-modal retrieval, pose estimation, and pose editing.

name: SAR3D
tokenizer: Multi-scale 3D VQVAE (hierarchical discrete 3D latent codes)
task: 3D Object Generation, 3D Object Understanding (captioning), Text-to-3D Generation
dataset:
Training: G-Objaverse, 3DTopia
Baselines compared: Splatter-Image, OpenLRM, Shap-E, LN3Diff
metric: FID, KID (generation quality), MUSIQ (rendering quality), COV, MMD (diversity), Latency (V100)
highlight: SAR3D proposes a multi-scale 3D VQVAE for hierarchical 3D object tokenization, enabling scalable autoregressive 3D generation and understanding within a single GPT-style transformer trained on truncated token sequences for efficiency.

name: MUSE-VL
tokenizer: Image encoder: CNN + CLIP; Image decoder: ConvNet + Transformer
task: Visual Question Answering, Image Reasoning, Science QA, Text-to-Image Generation
dataset:
Training: ImageNet-1K + CC12M (pretraining); visual instruction tuning data and image caption data
Benchmarks: MMBench, MMStar, SEED, MMMU, SQA-I, AI2D, MathVista (understanding); MJHQ-30K, GenEval (generation)
metric: Average Accuracy, FID, Alignment Score (GenEval)
highlight: MUSE-VL is a scalable unified multimodal LLM supporting both visual understanding and generation, demonstrating that strong LLM backbones (Qwen-2.5, Yi-1.5) with a CNN+CLIP tokenizer outperform specialized models across reasoning and generation tasks.

name: AIMV2
tokenizer: ViT with masked autoencoding (MAE-style pretraining); SwiGLU FFN + RMSNorm
task: Image Recognition, Object Detection and Grounding, Visual Question Answering, Image Captioning
dataset:
Training: Proprietary dataset from DFN-2B + COYO + HQITP (with alt-text and synthetic captions)
Benchmarks: iNAT-18, CIFAR-10/100, Food101, DTD, Pets, Cars (recognition); COCO, LVIS, RefCOCO/+/g (detection/grounding); GQA, OKVQA, TextVQA, DocVQA, ChartQA, SEED (multimodal QA); COCO, TextCaps, NoCaps (captioning)
metric: IN-1K Accuracy, mAP (detection), VQA Accuracy, CIDEr (captioning)
highlight: AIMv2 trains a multimodal autoregressive image model using joint image and text generation objectives, achieving state-of-the-art visual representation learning that transfers strongly to recognition, grounding, and multimodal understanding tasks.

name: LLaMA-Mesh
tokenizer: LLaMA tokenizer (3D mesh vertices/faces represented as plain text OBJ format)
task: Text-to-3D Mesh Generation, 3D Mesh Understanding, Interleaved Text and 3D Generation
dataset:
Training: Objaverse (3D meshes), UltraChat (conversational text)
Benchmarks: MMLU, PIQA, HellaSwag, GSM8K (language preservation); qualitative mesh quality evaluation
metric: MMLU/PIQA/HellaSwag/GSM8K Accuracy (language), Mesh Quality (qualitative), GPU Training Hours
highlight: LLaMA-Mesh enables a pretrained LLM to generate and understand 3D meshes by representing OBJ files as plain text tokens, requiring no architectural changes and preserving the LLM's language capabilities while gaining 3D generation ability.

name: JanusFlow
tokenizer: Understanding: SigLIP (continuous visual features); Generation: Rectified Flow encoder (continuous latents)
task: Text-to-Image Generation, Visual Question Answering, Image Captioning
dataset:
Training (understanding): DetailedCaption, SAM, arXivQA, DenseFusion-1M, MMSci, PixelProse, LAION-Aesthetics (recaptioned), Open Images V4 (recaptioned), ShareGPT4V, WikiHow, WIT
Training (generation): LAION-Aesthetics (recaptioned), DALL-E 3 1M, SAM, Open Images V4, Megalith-10M, YFCC-15M, PixelProse, JourneyDB
Benchmarks: GenEval, DPG-Bench (generation); POPE, MME, MMBench, SEED, VQAv2, GQA, MM-Vet, MMMU, ChartQA, TextVQA (understanding)
metric: FID, CLIP Score (generation), Accuracy (VQA benchmarks)
highlight: JanusFlow decouples visual encoding for understanding (SigLIP) and generation (Rectified Flow) within a single LLM, achieving unified image understanding and high-quality generation by eliminating the representational conflict between the two tasks.

name: MoTe
tokenizer: VAE (motion sequence encoder/decoder)
task: Text-to-Motion Generation, Motion-to-Text Generation, Text Variation, Motion Variation
dataset:
Training: HumanML3D, KIT Motion-Language Dataset
Benchmarks: HumanML3D test split, KIT test split
metric: R-Precision, MMDist (multimodal distance), FID, MModality (diversity), BLEU, ROUGE, CIDEr, BERTScore
highlight: MoTe proposes a unified motion-text diffusion model supporting bidirectional motion-text generation (text-to-motion, motion-to-text) and variation tasks, using a multi-task diffusion objective with a shared Motion-Text Diffusion Model (MTDM).

name: Freeze-Omni
tokenizer: Speech encoder: conv downsampling + Transformer blocks; Speech decoder: NAR decoder + AR decoder + codec model decoder
task: Spoken Question Answering, Automatic Speech Recognition, Speech Dialogue
dataset:
Training: Proprietary speech-text instruction data (LLM frozen throughout)
Benchmarks: AISHELL-1, WenetSpeech test-net/meeting (CER); LibriSpeech dev/test-clean/other (WER); Llama-Questions, Web Questions, TriviaQA (SQA)
metric: CER (Chinese ASR), WER (English ASR), Accuracy (spoken QA), Latency (ms)
ps: Key insight: classic Encoder+Adapter+LLM+Decoder paradigm where the LLM backbone remains completely frozen, preserving text-domain intelligence in the speech modality.
highlight: Freeze-Omni keeps the LLM backbone entirely frozen during training, attaching only lightweight speech encoder/decoder adapters, ensuring the speech-mode intelligence matches the text-mode capability of the underlying LLM without any parameter degradation.

name: CELL-Diff
tokenizer: ESM2 (protein sequence encoder)
task: Protein Subcellular Localization Image Prediction, Protein Sequence Generation, Localization Signal Generation
dataset:
Training: Human Protein Atlas, OpenCell
Benchmarks: Human Protein Atlas (held-out test), OpenCell test split
metric: IoU (localization prediction), FID (image quality), MSF-Resolvability (sequence validity)
highlight: CELL-Diff is a multimodal diffusion model for joint protein sequence and subcellular localization image generation, enabling bidirectional prediction between protein sequences and their fluorescent microscopy cell images.

name: Janus
tokenizer: Understanding: SigLIP (continuous features); Generation: VQ tokenizer (discrete tokens)
task: Visual Question Answering, Image Captioning, Text-to-Image Generation
dataset:
Training (understanding): ShareGPT4V, WikiHow, WIT; Training (generation): ImageNet-1K, MSCOCO, MJHQ-30K
Benchmarks: MMBench, SEED-Bench, POPE, MMMU, MM-Vet (understanding); MSCOCO-30K, GenEval, MJHQ-30K (generation)
metric: Accuracy (VQA), FID, GenEval Compositional Score
highlight: Janus decouples the visual encoding pathway into separate encoders for understanding (SigLIP, high-level semantics) and generation (VQ tokenizer, fine-grained details) while sharing a single unified autoregressive transformer, resolving the representational conflict in prior unified models.

name: PUMA
tokenizer: Visual encoder: CLIP; Image generation: SDXL-VAE
task: Text-to-Image Generation, Image Editing, Conditional Image Generation, Visual Question Answering
dataset:
Training (pretraining): LAION-2B, LAION-Aesthetics, GRIT, The Pile, OCR-VQA-200K, LLaVAR
Training (instruction tuning): LAION-Aesthetics, JourneyDB, SEED-Edit, MultiGen-20M, LLaVA-OneVision, CamBrain
Benchmarks: MSCOCO-30K (T2I); Emu-Edit (image editing); MMBench, MME, GQA, VQAv2, POPE, VizWiz (understanding)
metric: PSNR, CLIP-I, CLIP-T, LPIPS, DINO (editing), Accuracy, Recall (VQA)
highlight: PUMA unifies diverse multimodal generation and understanding tasks (T2I generation, image editing, conditional generation, and VQA) within a single LLaMA-3 model using a combination of diffusion decoder and autoregressive text generation.

name: Vitron
io: I, V, T → I, V, T
tokenizer: CLIP-ViT (visual encoder)
task: Visual Comprehension, Text-to-Image/Video Generation, Video/Image Segmentation, Image/Video Editing
dataset:
Training: CC3M, WebVid, RefCOCO/+/g, Flickr30K Entities, LaSOT, GOT10K, DanceTrack, MOT16
Benchmarks: RefCOCO/+/g (segmentation); DAVIS-17, YouTube-VOS-2019 (video segmentation); OKVQA, GQA, VSR, IconVQA, VizWiz, HMMS (image QA); VD-QA, MSRVTT-QA, TGIF-QA, ActivityNet-QA, VidSTG, HC-STVG (video QA)
metric: METEOR, CIDEr (captioning), mIoU, J&F, J, F (segmentation), Accuracy (QA), FID, CLIP-SIM (generation), FVD, IS (video generation)
highlight: Vitron is a universal pixel-level video/image understanding and generation MLLM supporting fine-grained tasks including segmentation, tracking, editing, and generation within a unified framework, with both spatial and temporal pixel-level comprehension capabilities.

name: UniMuMo
io: T, Mu, Mo → T, Mu, Mo
tokenizer: Music: EnCodec (RVQ-VAE); Motion: encoded via music codebook (mapped into music feature space)
task: Text-to-Music Generation, Text-to-Motion Generation, Music-to-Motion Generation, Motion-to-Music Generation, Music Captioning, Motion Captioning, Joint Music+Motion Generation
dataset:
Training: Music4All, MusicCaps, AIST++, HumanML3D
Benchmarks: MusicCaps (Text→Music); AIST++ (Motion→Music, Music→Motion, Text→Motion); MusicQA, HumanML3D (Music/Motion→Text)
metric: FAD, KL Divergence, CLAP Score (music generation); Beats Coverage, Beats Hit (rhythm alignment); Dist, Beat Align (motion quality); BLEU, METEOR, ROUGE-L, BERTScore, R-Precision, MM-Dist, CIDEr (captioning)
highlight: UniMuMo is the first unified model for arbitrary-to-arbitrary generation across text, music, and motion, encoding motion sequences with a music codebook to bridge modalities and enabling joint music-motion generation through a single encoder-decoder Transformer with one unified training objective.

name: Emu3
io: I, V, T → I, V, T
tokenizer: Vision/Video: SBER-MoVQGAN (discrete VQ tokenizer); Text: standard BPE tokenizer
backbone: LLaMA-2-based decoder-only Transformer trained from scratch with next-token prediction
task: Text-to-Image Generation, Text-to-Video Generation, Video Prediction, Visual Question Answering, Image Captioning, Multi-modal Understanding
dataset:
Training: Aquila (language), proprietary image-text pairs, proprietary video data
Benchmarks: SEEDBench-Img, OCRBench, MMVet, POPE, VQAv2, GQA, TextVQA, ChartQA, AI2D, RealWorldQA, MMMU, MMBench (understanding); VBench (video generation); MSCOCO-30K, GenEval, T2I-CompBench, DPG-Bench (image generation)
metric: CLIP-I, CLIP-T, FID (image generation on MSCOCO); Overall Score (GenEval); Color, Shape, Texture (T2I-CompBench); DSG Score (DPG-Bench); Accuracy (understanding benchmarks)
highlight: Emu3 demonstrates that a single Transformer trained with next-token prediction on tokenized images, text, and videos can match or surpass specialized task-specific models, eliminating the need for diffusion models, CLIP, or separate encoders for multimodal generation and understanding.

name: MIO
io: I, T, A, V → I, T, A, V
tokenizer: Image: SEED-Tokenizer (discrete VQ); Speech: SpeechTokenizer (RVQ-based)
task: Image Captioning, Visual Question Answering, Text-to-Image Generation, Video Understanding, Text-to-Video Generation, Automatic Speech Recognition, Text-to-Speech Synthesis, Language Modeling, Visual Storytelling, Instructional Image Editing, Interleaved Video-Text Generation, Multimodal In-Context Learning
dataset:
Pretraining: SBU, CC3M, LAION-COCO, JourneyDB (image-text); RefinedWeb (text); OBELICS, MMC4-core-ff (interleaved); WebVid-10M (video-text); HowTo-100M, YouTube-Temporal-180M (video interleaved); Libriheavy (speech-text)
SFT: OpenHermes, MMICL, ScienceQA, Geo170K, InstructPix2Pix, MagicBrush, LibriSpeech, GigaSpeech, Common Voice, VideoChat2-IT, Vision-Flan, LLaVAR, VIST, and others
Benchmarks: MSCOCO, Flickr30K (captioning); VQAv2, OK-VQA, VizWiz, SEED-Bench (VQA); MSCOCO, Flickr30K (image generation); LibriSpeech (ASR); VCTK, LibriSpeech test-clean, GLOBE (TTS); MSVD-QA, MSRVTT-QA (video QA); MMLU (language)
metric: CIDEr (captioning); VQA Accuracy, MCQ Accuracy (visual understanding); CLIP-I, SSIM, Aesthetic Score (image generation); WER (ASR); WER, Speaker Similarity (TTS)
highlight: MIO is a novel any-to-any multimodal foundation model supporting text, image, speech, and video understanding and generation in a unified causal multimodal sequence modeling framework, trained with a four-stage curriculum that progressively builds interleaved multimodal reasoning capability.

name: UniMotion
tokenizer: SMPL body pose parameters (axis-angle representation); text encoded with CLIP ViT-B/32
task: Text-to-Motion Generation, Motion-to-Text Captioning, Unconditional Motion Generation, Frame-Level Motion Understanding, Hierarchical Motion Generation (global + local conditioning)
dataset:
Training: BABEL (frame-level annotations), HumanML3D (sequence-level annotations)
Benchmarks: BABEL, HumanML3D
metric: R-Precision@1/2/3, MM-Dist, FID, Diversity (motion quality); BLEU-1/2/3/4, ROUGE-L, CIDEr, BERTScore (text generation); Beat Alignment (rhythm alignment)
ps:
Project page: https://coral79.github.io/uni-motion/
Mo = Motion (human body motion sequences, not music).
highlight: UniMotion is the first unified framework for both hierarchical text-to-motion synthesis and frame-level motion-to-text generation, using a dual-timestep diffusion mechanism that jointly denoises motion and text with separate timesteps per modality, enabling flexible global, local, and combined conditioning.

name: MonoFormer
tokenizer: Image: VAE (continuous latent for diffusion); Text: standard tokenizer (TinyLlama BPE)
task: Text-to-Image Generation, Text Generation, Visual Question Answering, Multimodal Understanding
dataset:
Training: UltraChat (SFT fine-tuning)
Benchmarks: ImageNet, JourneyDB (image generation); UltraChat, HellaSwag, OpenBookQA, WinoGrande, ARC-Easy, ARC-Challenge, BoolQ, PIQA (language understanding)
metric: FID, Inception Score (IS), Precision, Recall (image generation); Accuracy (language benchmarks)
highlight: MonoFormer proposes sharing a single Transformer backbone for both autoregressive text generation and diffusion-based image generation, demonstrating that the same model weights can effectively serve two fundamentally different generative paradigms without performance degradation.

name: Qwen2-VL
tokenizer: Vision: ViT with 2D-RoPE (Naive Dynamic Resolution — variable image tokens per resolution); Text: Qwen2 BPE tokenizer
parameter: Qwen2-VL-2B: 1.5B LLM + 675M ViT; Qwen2-VL-7B: 7.6B LLM + 675M ViT; Qwen2-VL-72B: 72B LLM + 675M ViT
task: Visual Question Answering, Document Understanding, OCR, Multilingual Text Recognition, Mathematical Reasoning, Referring Expression Comprehension, Video Understanding, Visual Grounding, UI Automation, Robotic Control, Vision-Language Navigation
dataset:
Training: proprietary cleaned web pages, open-source datasets, and synthetic data
Benchmarks: RealWorldQA, MMStar, MMVet, MMT-Bench, MMBench-1.1, MME, HallusionBench (general VQA); DocVQA, InfoVQA, ChartQA, TextVQA, AI2D, OCRBench (document/OCR); MTVQA (multilingual); MathVista, MathVision (math); RefCOCO/+/g (grounding); MVBench, PerceptionTest, EgoSchema, Video-MME (video); AITZ (UI), ALFRED (robotics), R2R, REVERIE (navigation)
metric: Accuracy, Exact Match, ANLS, Type Match, Goal Completion Rate, Success Rate
highlight: Qwen2-VL introduces Naive Dynamic Resolution encoding, which processes images at their native resolution into a variable number of visual tokens, combined with Multimodal Rotary Position Embedding (M-RoPE) for unified positional reasoning across text, images, and video frames in a single model.

name: LLaMA-Omni
tokenizer: Speech encoder: Whisper-large-v3; Speech decoder: custom streaming CTC-based decoder
task: Speech Instruction Following, Speech-to-Text Response Generation, Speech-to-Speech Response Generation, Real-Time Speech Interaction
dataset:
Training: InstructS2S-200K (200K speech instruction-response pairs, constructed by authors)
Benchmarks: InstructS2S-Eval (speech-to-text and speech-to-speech instruction following)
metric: ChatGPT Score (response quality); WER (speech accuracy); UTMOS (naturalness); Response Latency
highlight: LLaMA-Omni integrates a Whisper speech encoder, a speech adaptor, an LLM, and a streaming CTC speech decoder to simultaneously generate text and speech responses from speech input without requiring ASR transcription, achieving low-latency real-time interaction while constructing the InstructS2S-200K training corpus.

name: VILA-U
io: I, V, T → I, V, T
tokenizer: Vision: SigLIP-SO400M-patch14-384 (for understanding) + RQ-VAE (for generation); Text: standard BPE tokenizer
task: Visual Question Answering, Image Captioning, Video Understanding, Text-to-Image Generation, Text-to-Video Generation
dataset:
Training: COYO-700M (vision tower pretraining); ShareGPT4V (1M), MMC4 (6M) (understanding); 15M proprietary high-quality text-image pairs, OpenVid (1M) (generation)
Benchmarks: VQAv2, GQA, TextVQA, POPE, MME, SEED-Bench, MM-Vet (image understanding); ActivityNet, MSVD, MSRVTT, TGIF (video understanding); MJHQ-30K, GenAI-Bench (image generation); VBench (video generation)
metric: VQA Accuracy, CIDEr (understanding); FID, CLIP Score (image generation); VBench Score (video generation)
highlight: VILA-U unifies visual understanding and generation in a single autoregressive next-token prediction framework using a unified vision tower with RQ-VAE discrete tokens, eliminating separate diffusion models and achieving image generation quality competitive with diffusion models while maintaining strong understanding performance.

name: Mini-Omni
tokenizer: Audio encoder: Whisper (speech encoder); Audio decoder: parallel decoding module inspired by MusicGen
task: Real-Time Speech Interaction, Speech-to-Speech Dialogue, Automatic Speech Recognition, Text Response Generation
dataset:
Training: proprietary instruction-following speech dataset
Benchmarks: LibriSpeech (ASR evaluation)
metric: WER (ASR on LibriSpeech); Response Naturalness (qualitative); Latency
ps: "Any Model Can Talk" — training methodology for adding real-time speech interaction to existing LLMs with minimal language capability degradation.
highlight: Mini-Omni enables real-time end-to-end speech dialogue on a 0.5B model by combining a Whisper speech encoder with a text-instructed parallel speech decoding strategy, and proposes the "Any Model Can Talk" fine-tuning recipe to add streaming speech output to any LLM with minimal degradation to original language capabilities.

name: Show-o
tokenizer: Image understanding: CLIP-ViT (continuous tokens); Image generation: MAGVIT-v2 (discrete VQ tokens)
task: Visual Question Answering, Image Captioning, Text-to-Image Generation, Multimodal Understanding, Mixed-Modality Generation
dataset:
Training: RefinedWeb (text), ImageNet-1K (class-conditional images), CC12M, SA1B, LAION-Aesthetics-12M, DataComp, COYO-700M, JourneyDB (image-text)
Benchmarks: POPE, MME, Flickr30K, VQAv2, GQA, MMMU (understanding); MSCOCO, GenEval (generation)
metric: FID, CLIP-Score (image generation on MSCOCO); Overall Score (GenEval); VQA Accuracy, Recall (understanding benchmarks)
highlight: Show-o is a unified Transformer that handles both discrete autoregressive token prediction (for text and image understanding) and continuous diffusion-based denoising (for image generation) within a single model, using MAGVIT-v2 tokens for generation and CLIP-ViT features for understanding.

name: TransFusion
tokenizer: Image: VAE (continuous latent) + U-Net denoiser; Text: LLaMA-2 BPE tokenizer
backbone: Transformer (trained from scratch combining LLaMA-2 text and Stable Diffusion image objectives)
task: Text Generation, Text-to-Image Generation, Image Captioning, Image Editing, Bidirectional Image Noising/Denoising
dataset:
Training: LLaMA-2 text corpus; Shutterstock images, Conceptual 12M (image-text)
Benchmarks: Wikipedia, C4, LLaMA-2 Eval Suite (language); MS-COCO (captioning); GenEval (image generation); EmuEdit (image editing)
metric: Perplexity (language); CIDEr (captioning); FID, CLIP Score (image generation on MS-COCO); GenEval Overall Score; PSNR, SSIM (image editing)
highlight: TransFusion trains a single Transformer jointly on next-token prediction for text and diffusion-based denoising for images, inserting special BOI/EOI tokens to switch between language modeling and image diffusion modes, achieving strong performance on both language benchmarks and image generation without sacrificing either modality.

name: xGen-MM
tokenizer: Vision: SigLIP-SO400M-patch14-384 (variable-resolution ViT with any-resolution encoding)
task: Visual Question Answering, Image Captioning, Document Understanding, OCR, Referring Expression Comprehension, Multi-image Reasoning, In-Context Learning
dataset:
Training: COYO-700M, DataComp-1B, LAION-COCO (image-text); ShareGPT4V, InstructPix2Pix, LLaVA-Instruct, DocVQA, ChartQA (SFT)
Benchmarks: MME, MMBench, SEED-Bench, VQAv2, TextVQA, DocVQA, POPE, HallusionBench, MMMU, RealWorldQA
metric: VQA Accuracy, ANLS (document QA), CIDEr (captioning), MME Score, Hallucination Rate
ps: Also known as BLIP-3. Project page: https://www.salesforceairesearch.com/opensource/xGen-MM/index.html
highlight: xGen-MM (BLIP-3) is an open framework for large multimodal models featuring a scalable any-resolution visual encoding strategy and a meticulously curated multi-stage training recipe, producing a suite of vision-language models from 3.8B to 40B that match or exceed proprietary models on document understanding and multi-image reasoning tasks.

name: UnifiedMLLM
io: I, T, V, A → I, T, V, A
tokenizer: Vision encoder: CLIP-ViT; Modality-specific generation heads for image, video, and audio
task: Referring Segmentation, Reasoning-based Image Editing, Layout-based Image Generation, Text-to-Image Generation, Text-to-Video Generation, Text-to-Audio Generation, Video Segmentation, Video Editing, Image-Audio-to-Video Generation
dataset:
Training: RefCOCO/+/g (segmentation), Reason-Edit (reasoning editing), NSR-1K (layout generation), COCO-Caption, MSR-VTT, AudioCaps (multimodal generation)
Benchmarks: RefCOCO/+/g (segmentation); Reason-Edit (editing); NSR-1K (layout); COCO-Caption (image gen); MSR-VTT (video gen); AudioCaps (audio gen)
metric: cIoU (segmentation); PSNR, SSIM, CLIP Score (image editing); Precision, Recall, Accuracy, GLIP Score, CLIP Similarity (layout generation); FID, CLIPSIM (image/video generation); Frechet Distance, IS (audio generation)
highlight: UnifiedMLLM is a single MLLM that handles diverse tasks across text, image, video, and audio modalities — including segmentation, reasoning-based editing, layout generation, and cross-modal generation — by routing tasks to modality-specific expert generation heads via a unified instruction-following interface.

name: TextHarmony
tokenizer: Vision understanding: CLIP-ViT; Image generation: VQ-VAE (discrete visual tokens)
task: Visual Text Comprehension (OCR, Document QA, Chart Understanding), Visual Text Generation, Visual Text Editing, Scene Text Recognition
dataset:
Training: MARIO-LAION (visual text generation), DocStruct4M (document understanding)
Benchmarks: InfoVQA, DocVQA, ChartQA, TabFact, WTQ, TextVQA, OCRVQA, STVQA, OCRBench (comprehension); AnyText-Benchmark-EN, MARIOEval (generation)
metric: Accuracy, NED (Normalized Edit Distance) (text recognition/comprehension); FID, CLIP Score (text generation)
highlight: TextHarmony unifies visual text comprehension and generation in a single model using a Slide-LoRA module that adaptively merges separate understanding and generation LoRA weights, enabling simultaneous state-of-the-art performance on both OCR/document comprehension and visual text generation without mutual interference.

name: ANOLE
tokenizer: Text: BPE (65,536 vocab); Image: VQ-VAE from Chameleon (512×512 → 1,024 tokens, 8,192-token codebook)
task: Interleaved Image-Text Generation, Visual Question Answering, Image Captioning, Instruction Following with Mixed-Modal Output
dataset:
Training: open-sourced instruction tuning dataset (proprietary, released by authors)
Benchmarks: MME, POPE, LLaVA-Bench-in-the-Wild, MS-COCO
metric: MME Score, Accuracy (POPE, hallucination); CIDEr (captioning); GPT-4 Win Rate (LLaVA-Bench)
highlight: ANOLE is an open-source, natively multimodal autoregressive model for interleaved image-text generation built on Chameleon, addressing the lack of open models with native (non-adapter-based) multimodal generation by releasing model weights, instruction tuning data, and a parameter-efficient fine-tuning framework.

name: GenLLaVA
tokenizer: Vision understanding: SigLIP; Image generation: VQ-VAE
task: Visual Question Answering, Image Captioning, Reasoning, Image Editing, Text-to-Image Generation
dataset:
Training: ShareGPT, LLaVA-Pretrain, LLaVA-Instruct, LRV-Instruction, LAION-GPT4V, ShareGPT4V, DocVQA, SynDog-EN, ChartQA, DVQA, AI2D; Instruction Prompt-to-Prompt dataset (editing)
Benchmarks: MMBench, SEED-Bench, MMVet, MathVista, MMMU (understanding); CC3M, MS-COCO, EVR (generation/editing)
metric: Accuracy (understanding benchmarks); FID, CLIP Similarity (image generation/editing)
highlight: GenLLaVA extends LLaVA with image generation capability by introducing a VQ-VAE-based discrete image generation head alongside the standard language modeling head, enabling a single model to perform both multimodal understanding and high-quality text-to-image generation without additional diffusion components.

name: VisionLLM v2
tokenizer: Vision: CLIP-ViT (understanding) + VQ-VAE (generation); Text: Vicuna tokenizer
task: Visual Question Answering, Image Captioning, Object Detection, Instance Segmentation, Semantic Segmentation, Interactive Segmentation, Referring Expression Comprehension, Visual Grounding, Region Captioning, Pose Estimation, Text-to-Image Generation, Instruction-based Image Editing, Visual Commonsense Reasoning
dataset:
Training: ShareGPT4V, All-Seeing, VQA, COCO, RefCOCO/+/g, LAION-Aesthetics, MMIC (proprietary)
Benchmarks: VQAv2, GQA, VizWiz, ScienceQA, TextVQA, POPE, MME, MMBench-EN/CN, SEED-Bench, MM-Vet (VQA/captioning); COCO, LVIS, PACO (detection); VCR (commonsense); COCO, CrowdHuman (detection); COCO, CrowdPose, AP-10K, Human-Art (pose); RefCOCOg, Visual Genome (grounding); ADE20K (segmentation); COCO-Interactive (interactive seg)
metric: Accuracy, VQA Score (understanding); mAP, AP50 (detection); mIoU, cIoU, gIoU (segmentation); AP (pose estimation); P@0.5, S-IoU (grounding); FID, CLIP Score (generation)
highlight: VisionLLM v2 introduces a super link mechanism that routes task-specific queries from a general LLM to specialized expert models (detection, segmentation, generation) via a unified token-based interface, enabling a single model to handle 100+ vision-language tasks without task-specific fine-tuning.

name: X-VILA
io: I, T, V, A → I, T, V, A
tokenizer: Text: SentencePiece; Other modalities: ImageBind Transformer encoders; Decoders: VideoCrafter2 (video), Stable Diffusion 1.5 (image), AudioLDM (audio)
task: Cross-Modal Understanding, Video-to-Image Generation, Image+Audio-to-Video Generation, Video-to-Audio Generation, Image-to-Video Generation, Video-to-Video Translation, Audio-to-Video Generation, Long-Context Cross-Modal Generation
dataset:
Training: LLaVA-Pretrain, CC3M (image-text); WebVid (video-text); AudioCaps, WavCaps (audio-text); MMC4, ActivityNet Captions (interleaved); LLaVA, VideoChat, NExT-GPT, Alpaca (SFT)
Benchmarks: VQAv2, VizWiz, MMMU-val (understanding); WebVid, ActivityNet Captions subsets (cross-modal generation)
metric: X2A Score (cross-modal alignment); VQA Accuracy; CLIP-SIM, FID (generation quality)
highlight: X-VILA extends VILA to any-to-any multimodal generation across image, text, video, and audio by introducing cross-modal alignment embeddings between ImageBind encoders and task-specific diffusion decoders, enabling novel compositional generation capabilities such as image+audio-to-video synthesis.

name: Chameleon
tokenizer: Text: BPE tokenizer (65,536 vocab including 8,192 image tokens); Image: VQ-VAE (512×512 → 1,024 discrete tokens, 8,192-token codebook)
backbone: Early-fusion Transformer trained from scratch (no pretrained LLM backbone)
task: Text Generation, Text-to-Image Generation, Image Captioning, Visual Question Answering, Interleaved Image-Text Generation, Mixed-Modal Reasoning
dataset:
Training: LLaMA-2 and CodeLLaMA text data (2.9T tokens), 1.4B image-text pairs (1.5T tokens), 400B tokens of interleaved web text/image data; ~9.2T total pretraining tokens
Benchmarks: VQAv2, MS-COCO (captioning), text-only language benchmarks (LLaMA-2 evaluation suite), human evaluation against Gemini Pro and GPT-4V (mixed-modal generation)
metric: CIDEr (image captioning on COCO); VQA Accuracy; Perplexity (language modeling); Win Rate vs. Gemini Pro/GPT-4V (human evaluation)
highlight: Chameleon is a family of early-fusion token-based mixed-modal models from Meta AI that treats images and text as equal-first-class tokens within a single unified Transformer, enabling generation and reasoning over arbitrary interleaved image-text sequences without separate vision encoders or diffusion decoders.

name: Libra
tokenizer: Text: SentencePiece BPE; Image generation: VQGAN (discrete tokens); Image understanding: CLIP-ViT (continuous features)
parameter: 11.3B (7B LLM + 4B routed visual expert + 0.3B CLIP vision encoder)
task: Visual Question Answering, Image Captioning, Multimodal Benchmark Evaluation, Image Completion, Text-to-Image Generation
dataset:
Training: 50M image-text pairs from COYO-700M and CC12M; 500K from COCO (pretraining); 665K from LLaVA-Instruct (fine-tuning)
Benchmarks: VQAv2, OK-VQA, GQA, VizWiz, ScienceQA (VQA); NoCaps, Flickr30K, COCO (captioning); POPE, MME, MMBench, MMBench-CN, SEED-Bench, MM-Vet, MMVP (multimodal benchmarks)
metric: VQA Accuracy, F1 (VQA tasks); CIDEr, SPICE (image captioning); MME Score, Accuracy (multimodal benchmarks); FID, IS (image generation)
highlight: Libra introduces a decoupled vision system on top of an LLM, using a routed visual expert with a cross-modal bridge module that routes visual and language attention flows separately — enabling both autoregressive image generation (via VQGAN tokens) and strong multimodal understanding within a single model.

name: UniCode
tokenizer: Unified visual-language codebook (TVAE-style VQ tokenizer); Image decoder: VQGAN; Text: LLaVA tokenizer
task: Text-to-Image Generation, Image Reconstruction, Visual Question Answering, Image Captioning
dataset:
Training: Mixed-665K (instruction tuning), CC3M (image-text pairs)
Benchmarks: ImageNet, LSUN-Cat, LSUN-Church, LSUN-Bedroom (image generation); ImageNet, LCS-558K (reconstruction); VQAv2, VizWiz, ScienceQA-IMG, TextVQA, POPE, MMBench, MMBench-CN (VQA)
metric: FID, Inception Score (IS), CLIP Score (image generation); Reconstruction FID (rFID) (image reconstruction); Accuracy (VQA)
highlight: UniCode learns a unified visual-language codebook through a language-driven iterative training paradigm with an "image decompression" pretraining task, enabling a single model to tokenize visual and text signals into a shared discrete space for both multimodal understanding and non-linguistic visual generation.

name: GPT-4o
io: I, T, A, V → I, T, A
tokenizer: End-to-end unified tokenizer across text, vision, and audio (proprietary)
backbone: Proprietary Transformer architecture (trained end-to-end across all modalities)
task: Visual Question Answering, Image Captioning, Optical Character Recognition, Code Generation, Mathematical Reasoning, Speech Recognition, Speech Synthesis, Real-Time Audio Conversation, Video Understanding, Image Generation
dataset:
Training: Proprietary large-scale multimodal dataset (undisclosed)
Benchmarks: MMMU, MathVista, HumanEval, GSM8K, MGSM, MMAU, MMTE, EvalBench (safety), VoiceBench, various language understanding benchmarks
metric: Accuracy (reasoning/VQA); WER (speech); HumanEval pass@1 (code); FID/CLIP Score (image generation); Average response latency (232–320ms)
ps: OpenAI. Technical report / system card published later than release date.
highlight: GPT-4o is OpenAI's first end-to-end omni model trained jointly across text, vision, and audio in a single neural network, enabling real-time audio conversation with ~320ms latency and natively generating text, audio, and image outputs from any combination of multimodal inputs.

name: WorldGPT
tokenizer: Vision encoder: CLIP-ViT; Text: standard LLM BPE tokenizer
task: World State Transition Prediction, Multimodal Instruction Following, Long-Horizon Task Planning, Visual Reasoning
dataset:
Training: millions of videos across diverse real-life domains (proprietary)
Benchmarks: WorldNet (multimodal state transition prediction benchmark, introduced by authors)
metric: Accuracy on WorldNet state transition prediction; GPT-4 evaluation score (instruction following quality)
highlight: WorldGPT is a generalist world model built on an MLLM augmented with a cognitive architecture combining memory offloading, knowledge retrieval, and context reflection, enabling accurate multimodal state transition prediction and long-horizon task planning while generating synthetic instruction data that matches the quality of real human-annotated data.

name: SEED-X
tokenizer: Image understanding: ViT-based visual encoder (CLIP ViT-H); Image generation: SDXL-based diffusion decoder
task: Visual Question Answering, Image Captioning, Multi-Granularity Image Generation, Image Editing, Interleaved Image-Text Comprehension
dataset:
Training: LAION-COCO (re-captioned), SAM (re-captioned), Unsplash, LAION-Aesthetics, JourneyDB, CapFusion, GRIT, MMC4, OBELICS, OpenFlamingo, LLaVAR, Wikipedia (pretraining); LLaVAR, MIMIC-IT, MathQA, ChartQA, AI2D, ScienceQA, KVQA, DVQA, LLaVA-150K, ShareGPT, InstructPix2Pix, MagicBrush, VIST, VITON-HD (instruction tuning)
Benchmarks: VQAv2, GQA, POPE, MME, SEED-Bench, MMBench, MM-Vet, MMMU (understanding); GenEval (image generation)
metric: VQA Accuracy, MME Score, GenEval Overall Score, Benchmark-specific Accuracy
highlight: SEED-X is a unified foundation model that supports multi-granularity image generation (from thumbnail-level to high-resolution region-level) alongside comprehensive multimodal understanding, using de-tokenization with SDXL to handle images of arbitrary sizes and aspect ratios without resolution-specific fine-tuning.

name: GiT
tokenizer: Image: ViT patch embedding; Text output: language modeling head (autoregressive BPE decoding)
backbone: Vanilla ViT (no task-specific modules or separate detection/segmentation heads)
task: Image Captioning, Visual Question Answering, Object Detection, Instance Segmentation, Referring Expression Comprehension, Surface Normal Estimation, Depth Estimation, Human Pose Estimation
dataset:
Training: COCO Caption, VQAv2, RefCOCO/+/g, COCO Detection, ADE20K, Taskonomy (joint multi-task training across 27 datasets)
Benchmarks: COCO (captioning, detection, segmentation); VQAv2, GQA (VQA); RefCOCO/+/g (grounding); NYUv2 (depth); MPII (pose)
metric: CIDEr (captioning); VQA Accuracy; AP, AP50 (detection/segmentation); cIoU, gIoU (grounding); RMSE, AbsRel (depth); PCK (pose)
highlight: GiT replaces all task-specific heads (bounding box regressors, pixel decoders, segmentation masks) with a single vanilla ViT and a universal language interface, enabling autoregressive text decoding to unify image-level, sparse, and dense prediction tasks in one model jointly trained across 27 vision datasets.

name: AnyGPT
io: I, T, A, Mu → I, T, A, Mu
tokenizer: Image: SEED Tokenizer (discrete VQ); Speech: SpeechTokenizer (RVQ); Music: EnCodec (RVQ); Generation decoders: SDXL (image), SpeechTokenizer decoder (speech), EnCodec decoder (music)
task: Image Captioning, Text-to-Image Generation, Automatic Speech Recognition, Text-to-Speech Synthesis, Music Captioning, Text-to-Music Generation, Any-to-Any Multimodal Dialogue
dataset:
Training: LAION-2B, LAION-COCO, LAION-Aesthetics, JourneyDB, MMC4 (image-text); GigaSpeech, Common Voice, MLS (ASR); YouTube-Music-1M, MusicGen-Synthesis (music); AnyInstruct-108K (proprietary multimodal instruction dataset)
Benchmarks: MS-COCO 2014 (captioning, generation); LibriSpeech (ASR); VCTK (TTS); MusicCaps (music captioning/generation)
metric: CIDEr (image captioning); CLIP Score (text-to-image); WER (ASR); WER, Speaker Similarity (TTS); CLAP Score (music captioning and generation)
highlight: AnyGPT is an any-to-any multimodal language model that discretizes speech, music, and images into tokens using modality-specific tokenizers (SpeechTokenizer, EnCodec, SEED), enabling unified next-token prediction training and constructing the AnyInstruct-108K multimodal instruction dataset for comprehensive any-to-any dialogue.

name: LWM
io: I, V, T → I, V, T
tokenizer: Visual tokenizer: VQGAN (discrete tokens); Text tokenizer: Byte-Pair Encoding (BPE)
backbone: LLaMA-2-7B (extended to 1M token context via RingAttention)
task: Long-Context Document QA, Long Video Understanding, Image Understanding, Video QA, Image Generation, Video Generation
dataset:
Training: UltraChat (language), proprietary long video dataset (1M token context training)
Benchmarks: LOFT (long-context retrieval: Quora, HotpotQA, NQ); Video-MME (long video understanding); VQAv2, GQA, ScienceQA (image understanding); MSVD, MSRVTT, TGIF (short video QA)
metric: Retrieval Accuracy (long-context); VQA Accuracy, Score (video/image QA); Perplexity (generation quality)
highlight: LWM scales a 7B model to 1M token context using RingAttention distributed training, enabling video and text understanding exceeding 1 hour of video or entire books, the first open-source model to handle such long multimodal sequences natively.

name: Video-LaVIT
io: I, V, T → I, V, T
tokenizer: Image: EVA-CLIP ViT-G/14 (visual encoder); Video: proprietary motion vector tokenizer (keyframe + temporal motion decomposition); Text: LLaMA-2 BPE
task: Visual Question Answering, Image Captioning, Video QA, Video Understanding, Text-to-Video Generation, Long Video Generation
dataset:
Training: proprietary video-image-text dataset (keyframe + motion vector decomposed)
Benchmarks: VQAv2, GQA, VizWiz, ScienceQA-IMG, MME, MMBench, SEED-Bench, MM-Vet (image understanding); MSVD-QA, MSRVTT-QA, ActivityNet-QA (video QA); PerceptionTest, EgoSchema (video understanding); MSR-VTT, UCF-101 (text-to-video generation); EvalCrafter (long video generation)
metric: VQA Accuracy, MME Score (image understanding); Accuracy, Score (video QA); CLIPSIM, FVD, FID, IS (video generation); KVD (long video generation)
highlight: Video-LaVIT decomposes videos into keyframes and temporal motion vectors, tokenizing them separately with a motion vector tokenizer, enabling an LLM to understand and generate videos as a few compact discrete tokens in a unified image-video-text generative pretraining framework.

name: MM-Interleaved
tokenizer: Image understanding: CLIP-ViT; Image generation: VQ-VAE (discrete visual tokens)
task: Image Captioning, Visual Question Answering, Visual Dialogue, Text-to-Image Generation, Interleaved Image-Text Comprehension and Generation, Referring Expression Comprehension, Segmentation-to-Image Translation, Visual Storytelling
dataset:
Pretraining: MMC4, LAION-2B, LAION-COCO, CC-12M, Objects365
SFT: LLaVA-Mix-665K, COCO Caption, VQAv2, ChartQA, DocVQA, EST-VQA, InfoVQA, STVQA, TextCaps, LLaVAR, OCR-VQA, DVQA, RefCOCO/+/g, ADE20K, VIST, PororoSV, FlintstonesSV
Benchmarks: COCO, Flickr30K, NoCaps, I2Para (captioning); VQAv2, OKVQA, VizWiz, TextVQA (VQA); VisDial (visual dialogue); MS-COCO, LN-COCO (text-to-image); SEED-Bench-2 (interleaved); VIST, Pororo, Flintstones (visual storytelling); RefCOCO/+/g (grounding); ADE20K (segmentation)
metric: CIDEr, SPICE (captioning); VQA Accuracy (VQA); NDCG (dialogue); FID, CLIP-Sim (image generation); IoU Accuracy, mIoU (segmentation)
highlight: MM-Interleaved introduces a multi-scale semantic image representation that enables large language models to perceive interleaved image-text sequences at multiple granularities, jointly supporting interleaved comprehension and generation through a shared VQ-VAE token space without separate diffusion models.

name: AIM
tokenizer: ViT patch embedding (no discrete tokenization — predicts masked patch features autoregressively)
task: Image Classification, Transfer Learning across Diverse Visual Domains
dataset:
Training: DFN dataset (proprietary large-scale image dataset)
Benchmarks: ImageNet-1K, iNAT18, CIFAR-10, CIFAR-100, Food101, DTD, Oxford Pets, Stanford Cars, iWildCam, Camelyon17, PCAM, RxRx1, EuroSAT, fMoW, Infographic VQA
metric: Top-1 Accuracy (all classification benchmarks)
highlight: AIM is a family of vision-only autoregressive models (0.6B–6.5B) pretrained with next-patch prediction, demonstrating that LLM-style autoregressive pretraining on images yields consistent scaling laws: performance improves predictably with model capacity and data quantity, and the loss correlates with downstream transfer accuracy.

name: Uni3D-LLM
tokenizer: Point cloud: Point-BERT; Image: CLIP-ViT / ConvNeXt / DINOv2; Cross-modal: Q-Former
backbone: LLM (specific base not disclosed in paper)
task: 3D Object Generation, 3D Object Editing, 3D Perception (Classification, Detection), 3D Visual Grounding, 3D Scene QA, 3D Captioning
dataset:
Training: Scan2Inst, ScanQA, Scan2Cap, ScanNet, ShapeNet, Cap3D, Cap3Descript, MS-COCO, LN-COCO
Benchmarks: ScanQA (3D QA), Scan2Cap (3D captioning), ShapeNet (classification), NR3D (captioning), Cap3D, Cap3Descript (generation evaluation)
metric: Accuracy (classification/VQA); CIDEr, METEOR (captioning); mAP (detection); Acc@0.5, mAcc (grounding); FID (generation)
highlight: Uni3D-LLM integrates 3D point cloud understanding and generation with an LLM by projecting point clouds, images, and text into a unified representation space via a Q-Former bridge, enabling a single model to handle 3D perception, grounding, captioning, generation, and editing tasks without task-specific 3D architectures.

name: CM3Leon
tokenizer: Image: Make-a-Scene VQ tokenizer; Text: custom BPE tokenizer (vocab size 56,320, trained on OPT data)
task: Text-to-Image Generation, Image Captioning, Visual Question Answering, Instructable Image Editing (text-guided, image-to-image grounded, spatially grounded), Image Paragraph Captioning, Visual Dialogue
dataset:
Training: Licensed Shutterstock image-text pairs (retrieval-augmented); InstructPix2Pix-style editing data (600K), ControlNet-processed Shutterstock (7M), COCO/Openimage/Object365 (3M spatial grounding examples), OCR examples (200K)
Benchmarks: MS-COCO (FID, text-to-image); MS-COCO, Flickr30K, Localized Narratives (captioning); VQAv2, VizWiz, OKVQA, ScienceQA, VisDial (understanding)
metric: FID (image generation on MSCOCO); CIDEr (image/paragraph captioning); VQA Accuracy; NDCG (visual dialogue)
highlight: CM3Leon is the first multimodal model trained with the full LLM recipe (retrieval-augmented pretraining + supervised fine-tuning) applied to mixed image-text generation, achieving state-of-the-art text-to-image FID at 7B scale while also supporting instructable image editing through a multi-task SFT stage.

name: AudioPaLM
tokenizer: Text: SentencePiece; Audio: w2v-BERT / USM-v1 / USM-v2 (speech tokenizer producing discrete audio tokens)
backbone: PaLM-2 (8B, initialized from pretrained text-only weights)
task: Automatic Speech Recognition, Automatic Speech Translation, Speech-to-Speech Translation, Text-to-Speech Synthesis, Machine Translation
dataset:
Training: CoVoST2/CVSS, VoxPopuli (ASR+S2ST), CommonVoice 11, Conversational EsEn, YouTube ASR, WMT/TED TTS data, PaLM-MT TTS data
Benchmarks: CoVoST2 (AST, ASR); FLEURS (AST, 102 languages); VoxPopuli (ASR); CVSS (S2ST)
metric: BLEU (speech translation, machine translation); WER, CER (ASR); ASR-BLEU (S2ST via ASR pipeline)
highlight: AudioPaLM fuses PaLM-2 and AudioLM into a unified architecture for speech understanding and generation by initializing from PaLM-2's pretrained text weights, demonstrating that large-scale text pretraining directly benefits speech tasks including cross-lingual speech-to-speech translation across 100+ language pairs.

name: UniDiffuser
tokenizer: Image encoder: Stable Diffusion VAE + CLIP-ViT-B/32; Image decoder: Stable Diffusion VAE decoder; Text encoder: CLIP text encoder; Text decoder: GPT-2-based autoregressive decoder
backbone: U-ViT (952M unified Transformer for joint image-text diffusion)
task: Text-to-Image Generation, Image-to-Text Generation, Unconditional Image Generation, Unconditional Text Generation, Joint Image-Text Generation
dataset:
Training: LAION-5B (image-text pairs)
Benchmarks: MS-COCO (text-to-image, image captioning)
metric: FID, CLIP Score (text-to-image); CLIP Score (image-to-text); BLEU-1, BLEU-4 (text decoder evaluation)
highlight: UniDiffuser formulates unified multimodal generation as a multi-flow diffusion problem, training a single U-ViT model with separate diffusion timesteps per modality to handle all conditional and unconditional image-text generation tasks in one framework without any architectural modifications at inference time.

name: Unified-IO 2
io: I, A, T, 3D → I, A, T
tokenizer: Text: BPE; Image: ViT encoder + VQGAN decoder; Audio: AST encoder + VQGAN decoder; Action: custom action tokenizer
backbone: Proprietary encoder-decoder Transformer (3 sizes: L=1.1B, XL=3.2B, XXL=6.8B)
task: Natural Language Inference, Text-to-Image Generation, Text-to-Audio Generation, Visual QA, Video Understanding, Object Detection, Depth Estimation, 3D Detection, Action Generation, Image Captioning, Audio Classification, Audio Captioning, Robotic Action Prediction
dataset:
Training: RedPajama, C4, Wikipedia, FlanV2 (text); LAION-400M, CC3M, CC12M, RedCaps, OBELICS (image-text); YT-Temporal-1B, ACAV100M, AudioSet, WebVid-10M, HD-VILA-10M, Ego4D (video-audio); CroCo, Objaverse, ProcTHOR, Habitat (3D/embodied); M3IT, MIMIC-IT (instruction tuning)
Benchmarks: HellaSwag (NLI); TIFA, MS-COCO (text-to-image); AudioCaps (text-to-audio); SEED-Bench, MMBench, POPE (VQA); GRIT (general vision); VIMA-Bench (action); Kinetics-400, VATEX, MSR-VTT, MSVD (video); Objectron, nuScenes, Hypersim (3D); NYUv2 (depth)
metric: Accuracy (classification/VQA); RMSE, AbsRel (depth); CIDEr, ROUGE (captioning); FID (image generation); FAD, KL (audio generation); Success Rate (robotic tasks)
highlight: Unified-IO 2 is the first autoregressive model capable of understanding and generating across image, text, audio, and embodied action modalities in a single encoder-decoder Transformer, tokenizing all inputs/outputs into a shared semantic space and scaling to 6.8B parameters with strong performance across 100+ diverse tasks.

name: Emu2
tokenizer: Image: EVA-02-CLIP-E-plus (continuous features); Image generation: SDXL diffusion decoder (continuous latent via regression)
task: Visual Question Answering, Image Captioning, In-Context Multimodal Learning, Text-to-Image Generation, Grounded Text-to-Image Generation
dataset:
Training: CapsFusion-Grounded-100M, GRIT (grounded text-to-image); CapsFusion, LAION-Aesthetics, SA-1B, LAION-High-Resolution (text-to-image pretraining)
Benchmarks: VQAv2, OKVQA, TextVQA, MMBench, SEED-Bench, MM-Vet, MMMU (understanding); MS-COCO (image generation)
metric: VQA Accuracy (understanding); CLIP-I, CLIP-T, DINO (image generation quality); FID (MS-COCO generation)
highlight: Emu2 scales multimodal in-context learning to 33B parameters by using continuous SDXL latent regression for image generation (rather than discrete VQ tokens), achieving state-of-the-art few-shot visual understanding and enabling grounded text-to-image generation with precise object placement through dual grounding supervision.

name: Gemini 1.0
io: I, T, A, V → I, T
tokenizer: Text: SentencePiece; Image/Video: proprietary ViT-based encoder; Audio: USM-based encoder
backbone: Proprietary Transformer (3 sizes: Ultra, Pro, Nano)
task: Visual Question Answering, Image Captioning, Video Understanding, Automatic Speech Recognition, Code Generation, Mathematical Reasoning, Scientific Reasoning, Long-Document Understanding, Cross-Lingual Understanding
dataset:
Training: Proprietary large-scale multimodal web data, books, code, and scientific documents (undisclosed)
Benchmarks: MMLU (knowledge); HumanEval, MBPP (code); GSM8K, MATH (math); MMMU, VQAv2 (multimodal); WMT23 (translation); Natural Questions (QA); DROP (reasoning); Big-Bench Hard; LibriSpeech (ASR)
metric: Accuracy, Pass@1, Exact Match (reasoning/code); BLEU (translation); WER (ASR); Win Rate vs. GPT-4V (human preference)
highlight: Gemini 1.0 is Google's first natively multimodal model family trained end-to-end on text, image, audio, and video, with the Ultra variant achieving human-expert performance on MMLU (90.0%) and state-of-the-art results on 30 of 32 academic benchmarks at the time of release.

name: VL-GPT
tokenizer: Text encoder/decoder: LLaMA-2-7B BPE (extended with image tokens); Image: proprietary continuous encoder-decoder (image tokenizer-detokenizer) built on LLaMA-2 framework
task: Image Captioning, Visual Question Answering, Text-to-Image Generation, Multimodal Dialogue, Image Editing
dataset:
Training: CC3M, LAION-Aesthetics, LAION-COCO (image tokenizer training); MMC4, OBELICS (interleaved pretraining); LLaVA, SVIT (conversation SFT); COCO Caption, InstructPix2Pix, MagicBrush (task-specific tuning)
Benchmarks: MS-COCO (captioning, text-to-image); VQAv2, GQA, OKVQA, VizWiz (VQA); VisDial (visual dialogue)
metric: CIDEr (image captioning); VQA Accuracy (understanding); FID (text-to-image on MSCOCO); NDCG (visual dialogue)
highlight: VL-GPT introduces a unified image tokenizer-detokenizer framework that encodes images into continuous representations interleaved with text tokens in an LLM, enabling bidirectional image-text generation through autoregressive language modeling without requiring discrete visual codebooks or separate diffusion models.

name: LVM
tokenizer: VQGAN (image-to-discrete visual sentence tokens)
task: Semantic Segmentation, Depth Estimation, Surface Normal Estimation, Edge Detection, Video Frame Prediction, Object Rotation Prediction, Visual Prompting, Foreground Segmentation, Single Object Detection, Image Colorization
dataset:
Training: proprietary "visual sentences" dataset combining raw images, videos, semantic segmentations, depth maps, surface normals, and keypoints from ImageNet, Kinetics-700, and annotation sources
Benchmarks: ImageNet (classification via visual prompting); Kinetics-700 (video prediction); Pascal 3D+ (rotation/keypoint); COCO (segmentation, detection); NYUv2 (depth, colorization)
metric: Perplexity (generation); PCK (keypoint/pose); mIoU (segmentation); MSE, LPIPS (colorization); Top-1 Accuracy (classification)
highlight: LVM is the first large vision model trained exclusively on visual data without any language supervision, representing images, segmentation maps, depth maps, and videos as "visual sentences" in a unified VQGAN token space and learning visual in-context reasoning through autoregressive next-token prediction.

name: CoDi-2
io: I, T, A → I, T, A
tokenizer: Modality-specific encoders feeding into a shared LLM; Decoders: diffusion models per modality
task: Zero-Shot Cross-Modal Generation, Few-Shot In-Context Multimodal Learning, Subject-Driven Generation, Concept Composition, Multimodal Dialogue, Audio-Conditioned Generation, Image Editing
dataset:
Training: MIMIC-IT, LAION-400M, AudioSet, WebVid, InstructPix2Pix, Alpaca
Benchmarks: DreamBench (subject-driven generation); MS-COCO (image generation)
metric: CLIP-SIM (image similarity); Log Spectral Distance (LSD), Kullback-Leibler (KL), Frechet Distance (FD) (audio metrics); DreamBench Score (subject alignment)
highlight: CoDi-2 extends any-to-any generation with in-context learning capabilities, enabling zero-shot, one-shot, and few-shot cross-modal generation through exemplar learning and concept composition, allowing users to teach new concepts and style combinations across image, text, and audio modalities without fine-tuning.

name: ShapeGPT
tokenizer: Text: WordPiece; Visual: CLIP ViT-L/14; 3D Shape: VQ-VAE (discretizes continuous point clouds into shape words)
task: Text-to-Shape Generation, Shape-to-Text Captioning, Image-to-Shape Generation, Multimodal-to-Shape Generation, Shape Editing, Shape Completion, Shape Reasoning
dataset:
Training: ShapeNet, Objaverse, Text2Shape, custom ShapeWord dataset
Benchmarks: ShapeNet (3D generation quality), Text2Shape (text-shape alignment)
metric:
3D Generation: 3D-IoU, Chamfer Distance (CD), F-score@1%, ULIP Score
Captioning: CLIP Score
highlight: ShapeGPT introduces a word-sentence-paragraph framework to discretize continuous 3D shapes into compact shape tokens, enabling a pre-trained language model to jointly handle shape, image, and text within a unified autoregressive architecture. A three-stage training scheme (shape representation, multimodal alignment, instruction-based generation) aligns shape-language codebooks for cross-modal generation.

name: GPT4Video
tokenizer: Visual Encoder: CLIP ViT-L/14; Video Abstractor: custom cross-attention module with two learnable query tokens (compresses visual features)
task: Video Question Answering (Zero-shot), Video Captioning, Text-to-Video Generation, Harmful Content Detection (Safeguarding)
dataset:
Training: VideoChat-11K, GPT4Video-50K (custom instruction-tuning), custom safety dataset (sampled from Real-Toxicity-Prompts, 60 samples)
Benchmarks: MSRVTT-QA, MSVD-QA (Video QA); MSR-VTT (Video Captioning & Text-to-Video); custom NSFW benchmark
metric:
Video QA: Accuracy
Video Captioning: BLEU-4, METEOR
Text-to-Video Generation: FID, CLIP Similarity (CLIPSIM)
Harmful Content Detection: Accuracy, Precision, Recall, F1
highlight: GPT4Video presents a unified framework that integrates video understanding, generation, and safety filtering into a single system. A Video Abstractor module compresses visual features via cross-attention before feeding them to the LLM, and a dedicated safeguarding module detects harmful content in both inputs and generated outputs.

name: TEAL
io: I, T, A → I, T, A
tokenizer: Image: VQ-VAE (DALL-E / VQ-GAN / BEiT-V2 encoder); Audio: Whisper (small) k-means clustering or HuBERT clustering; Text: SentencePiece; De-tokenizers: corresponding VQ-VAE decoders
task: Image Captioning, Visual Question Answering (VQA), Automatic Speech Recognition (ASR), Image Generation
dataset:
Training: COCO 2014 (image-text), CoVoST 2 (ASR English), MNIST (image generation)
Benchmarks: COCO 2014 (captioning), ScienceQA (VQA), CoVoST 2 (ASR), MNIST (generation)
metric:
Image Captioning: CIDEr, BLEU-4
VQA: Accuracy
ASR: Word Error Rate (WER)
Image Generation: qualitative evaluation
highlight: TEAL (Tokenize and Embed ALl) treats inputs from any modality as discrete token sequences and learns a joint embedding space via a shared learnable embedding matrix. By converting all modalities into tokens, any pre-trained LLM can perform multimodal understanding and generation through standard autoregressive prediction without architectural changes.

name: SEED-LLaMA
