tokenizer: SEED tokenizer: discrete image tokens with 1D causal dependency, trained with a causal Q-Former and VQ codebook; image detokenizer: unCLIP decoder (SD image decoder)
task: Image Captioning, Visual Question Answering (VQA), Image Generation, Multimodal Dialogue, Visual Reasoning
dataset:
Pretraining: COCO Caption, CC3M, Unsplash, LAION-COCO, MMC4, OBELICS, WebVid
Instruction Tuning: LLaVA-Instruct, ShareGPT, custom multimodal instruction data
Benchmarks: POPE, MMBench, SEED-Bench, LLaVA-Bench, MM-Vet, COCO (captioning), VQAv2
metric:
VQA: Accuracy
Captioning: CIDEr
Multimodal Bench: MMBench Score, SEED-Bench Score, GPT-4 evaluation
Hallucination: POPE Accuracy
Image Generation: FID, CLIP Score
highlight: SEED-LLaMA introduces the SEED tokenizer, which encodes images into discrete tokens with 1D causal dependency aligned with the left-to-right autoregressive prediction of LLMs. This design bridges visual and textual representations, enabling a single LLM to both comprehend and generate images through standard next-token prediction.

name: MiniGPT-5
tokenizer: Encoder: CLIP ViT + Q-Former + linear layer; Decoder: Stable Diffusion U-Net; generative vokens bridge LLM outputs to the diffusion decoder
task: Unimodal Generation with Multimodal Input, Multimodal Interleaved Generation, Multimodal Story Generation, Multimodal Dialogue
dataset:
Training: CC3M (pre-training), VIST (visual storytelling fine-tuning), MMDialog (dialogue fine-tuning)
Benchmarks: VIST, MMDialog
metric:
Image Generation: CLIP-I, CLIP-T, IS, FID
Multimodal Generation: IS, BLEU-1, BLEU-2, Rouge-L, MM-Relevance
Story Generation: Human Evaluation
Narration: S-BERT, Rouge-L, METEOR
highlight: MiniGPT-5 introduces generative vokens — special learnable tokens produced by the LLM that condition a frozen Stable Diffusion decoder for image generation — enabling interleaved multimodal generation from a single LLM backbone. A two-stage training strategy (unimodal then multimodal) with classifier-free guidance allows controllable text and image outputs within a dialogue context.

name: Emu
tokenizer: Visual Encoder: CLIP ViT-L; Text Encoder: T5-XXL; Image Decoder: U-Net VAE
task: Text-to-Image Generation
dataset:
Training: ~1.1B curated image-text pairs (internal high-quality subset from LAION, COYO, CC)
Benchmarks: MS-COCO (FID), human preference study vs. SDXL v1.0
metric: FID (COCO-30K), Human Preference (Visual Appeal win rate)
ps: Meta AI. Diffusion-only model (not the same as Emu2).
highlight: Emu introduces quality-tuning, a supervised fine-tuning step on a small set of carefully curated high-aesthetic image-text pairs after standard pretraining, dramatically improving the visual appeal and prompt-following of the generated images. This simple post-training stage achieves an 82.9% win rate over the pretrained-only baseline and outperforms SDXL v1.0 on aesthetic preference.

name: DreamLLM
tokenizer: CLIP-Large (image encoder); Stable Diffusion U-Net (image decoder)
task: Image Captioning, Visual Question Answering (VQA), Text-related VQA, Multimodal Dialogue, Text-to-Image Generation, Interleaved Document Generation
dataset:
Training: LLaVA-Pretrain (558K), BLIP-LAION (8M), LAION-400M (11M), LAION-COCO (11M), MMC4 (2M), LLaVA-Instruct (80K/665K), InstructMMC4 (20K)
Benchmarks: COCO & Image2Paragraph (captioning); VQAv2, OKVQA, VizWiz, TextVQA (VQA); MMBench, MM-Vet, POPE (comprehensive); MS-COCO, LN-COCO (image generation); PIQA, HellaSwag, MMLU (NLP)
metric:
Captioning: CIDEr
VQA: Accuracy
Comprehensive: GPT-4 Eval (MM-Vet), GPT-3 Eval (MMBench)
Hallucination: Accuracy / F1 (POPE)
Image Generation: FID
NLP: Accuracy
highlight: DreamLLM directly synthesizes images as part of the LLM's generation process by embedding the diffusion objective into LLM pre-training, allowing the model to learn from naturally interleaved image-text documents. This joint training avoids modular alignment steps and enables free-form creation of interleaved image-text content in a single autoregressive pass.

name: NExT-GPT
io: I, V, A, T → I, V, A, T
tokenizer: ImageBind (unified multimodal encoder for all input modalities); Decoders: Stable Diffusion (image), Zeroscope (video), AudioLDM (audio)
task: Image/Video/Audio Captioning, Visual/Auditory Question Answering, Any-to-Any Multimodal Generation, Multimodal Dialogue
dataset:
Training: WebVid-2M, CC3M, AudioCaps, MosIT (custom multimodal instruction dataset)
Benchmarks: standard captioning and QA datasets for image, video, audio evaluation
metric:
Image Generation: FID
Audio Generation: FAD (Fréchet Audio Distance)
Video-Text Alignment: CLIP-SIM
Task-specific: Accuracy, BLEU, CIDEr
highlight: NExT-GPT is the first end-to-end any-to-any multimodal LLM that perceives and generates across all four major modalities (text, image, video, audio) using a single LLM backbone. Lightweight projection adapters connect frozen modality encoders and decoders to the LLM, enabling cross-modal instruction following with minimal trainable parameters.

name: LaVIT
tokenizer: Visual Encoder: EVA-CLIP ViT-G/14 + dynamic visual tokenizer (discretizes into visual tokens); Image Decoder: conditional U-Net (from Stable Diffusion)
task: Image Captioning, Visual Question Answering (VQA), Zero-shot Text-to-Image Generation
dataset:
Training: LAION-400M, LAION-5B, Conceptual Captions, SBU, BLIP-CapFilt, RedPajama
Benchmarks: NoCaps, Flickr30K (captioning); VQAv2, OKVQA, GQA, VizWiz (VQA); MS-COCO (text-to-image FID)
metric:
Captioning: CIDEr
VQA: Accuracy
Text-to-Image: FID
highlight: LaVIT proposes a dynamic visual tokenizer that translates non-linguistic images into a sequence of discrete tokens, enabling a pre-trained LLM to handle image and text indiscriminately under the same generative learning paradigm. Unlike fixed-grid tokenizers, the dynamic tokenizer adaptively selects the number of tokens per image region based on semantic complexity.

name: TokenFlow
tokenizer: Stable Diffusion VAE (encode/decode video frames)
task: Text-Driven Video Editing
dataset:
Evaluation: DAVIS video dataset (diverse natural videos)
Benchmarks: manual evaluation on DAVIS sequences
metric: Edit Fidelity (CLIP-based text-video alignment), Temporal Consistency (CLIP frame similarity)
highlight: TokenFlow enforces temporal consistency in text-driven video editing by explicitly propagating diffusion feature correspondences across frames rather than editing each frame independently. The key insight is that editing consistency can be achieved by sharing self-attention keys and values between frames based on inter-frame correspondences extracted from the source video.

name: GILL
tokenizer: CLIP ViT-L (image encoder); Stable Diffusion (image decoder via learned GILLMapper)
task: Image Captioning, Image-Text Retrieval, Image Generation, Multimodal Dialogue
dataset:
Training: CC3M
Benchmarks: CC3M test set, COCO (retrieval and generation)
metric: CLIP Similarity, LPIPS, FID
highlight: GILL (Generating Images with Large Language Models) introduces a lightweight GILLMapper network that maps LLM output embeddings to the CLIP text embedding space, enabling a frozen LLM to either retrieve relevant images from a corpus or generate new images via Stable Diffusion. This decision between retrieval and generation is learned end-to-end within the LLM.

name: Pengi
tokenizer: Audio Encoder: HTSAT (Hierarchical Token-Semantic Audio Transformer)
task: Audio Captioning, Audio Question Answering, Sound Event Classification, Acoustic Scene Classification, Speech Emotion Recognition, Speech Sentiment Analysis, Music Analysis, Instrument Classification, Vocal Sound Classification, Text-to-Audio Retrieval
dataset:
Training: AudioSet, FSD50K (sound events); CochlScene (scene); MSP Podcast, CMU MOSI/MOSEI, MELD (emotion/sentiment); NSynth, FMA (music); AudioCaps, ClothoV2 (captioning); ClothoAQA (QA); WavText5K, SoundDescs, MACS, WavCaps (auxiliary)
Benchmarks: Clotho, AudioCaps; ClothoAQA; ESC50, UrbanSound8K, DCASE2017 Task4; GTZAN, NSynth; CREMA-D, RAVDESS; Vocal Sound dataset
metric:
Captioning: SPIDEr (primary, CIDEr + SPICE), BLEU-1..4, METEOR, ROUGE-L, CIDEr, SPICE
Audio QA: Accuracy
Classification Tasks: Accuracy, mAP (multi-label), F1
Retrieval: Recall@1
highlight: Pengi frames all audio tasks as open-ended text generation, using an audio encoder and a text prefix to prompt a frozen GPT-2 language model. This transfer learning approach achieves state-of-the-art on 21 audio benchmarks spanning captioning, QA, classification, and retrieval without task-specific heads.

name: CoDi
io: I, V, A, T → I, V, A, T
tokenizer: Image LDM: Stable Diffusion 1.5 VAE; Video LDM: extended image LDM with temporal attention; Audio LDM: AudioLDM VAE; Text LDM: OPTIMUS (VAE-based text latent space)
backbone: Latent Diffusion Model (Stable Diffusion 1.5 + AudioLDM + OPTIMUS)
task: Any-to-Any Multimodal Generation: Single-Modality Generation (image, video, audio, text), Multi-Condition Generation, Multi-Output Joint Generation
dataset:
Training: LAION-400M (image-text), AudioSet + AudioCaps + Freesound 500K + BBC Sound Effects (audio-text), WebVid-10M + HD-VILA-100M (video), AudioSet + SoundNet (audio-visual)
Benchmarks: COCO-Caption (captioning), MSR-VTT (video), AudioSet + AudioCaps (audio), COCO (image generation)
metric:
Image Generation: FID
Video Generation: CLIP-SIM
Audio Generation: FAD, FD, IS; Semantic Alignment: OVL, REL
Captioning: BLEU-4, METEOR, CIDEr
Joint Generation: Cross-modal Cosine Similarity (SIM)
highlight: CoDi (Composable Diffusion) learns a set of modality-specific latent diffusion models and aligns their latent spaces via a shared cross-modal alignment objective, enabling any combination of input modalities to condition generation of any output modalities. A composable generation paradigm allows simultaneous multi-output synthesis (e.g., video + audio from text) without explicit paired training data for all combinations.

name: SpeechGPT
tokenizer: Speech Encoder: HuBERT (English) / mHuBERT (multilingual); Speech Decoder: HiFi-GAN unit vocoder
task: Automatic Speech Recognition (ASR), Text-to-Speech Synthesis (TTS), Spoken Dialogue, Cross-modal Instruction Following
dataset:
Stage 1 (Modality-Adaptation Pre-training): LibriLight (60K hours unlabelled English speech)
Stage 2 (Cross-modal Instruction Fine-Tuning): SpeechInstruct (custom, derived from GigaSpeech + Common Voice + LibriSpeech + moss-002-sft-data)
Stage 3 (Chain-of-Modality Fine-Tuning): moss-002-sft-data (converted to speech-text quadruplets)
Benchmarks: LibriSpeech (ASR), qualitative spoken dialogue evaluation
metric: Human Evaluation (instruction following, naturalness, coherence); WER (ASR subset)
highlight: SpeechGPT is the first large language model with intrinsic cross-modal conversational abilities, processing speech directly as discrete tokens rather than relying on a cascade ASR→LLM→TTS pipeline. A three-stage training curriculum (modality adaptation, cross-modal instruction tuning, chain-of-modality fine-tuning) enables the model to perceive and generate speech and text within a single autoregressive LLM.

name: Versatile Diffusion
tokenizer: VAE (image encoder/decoder); CLIP text encoder
backbone: Multi-flow latent diffusion network (shared VAE + modality-specific diffuser heads)
task: Text-to-Image Generation, Image-to-Text Generation, Image Variation, Text Variation, Style-Content Disentanglement, Dual-Context and Multi-Context Image Blending
dataset:
Training: LAION-2B-en + COYO-700M
Benchmarks: COCO-Caption (FID, user preference)
metric: FID, User Preference (human voting)
highlight: Versatile Diffusion unifies multiple image-text generation tasks (text-to-image, image-variation, image-to-text, text-variation) within a single multi-flow diffusion model by sharing a common VAE backbone and routing inputs through modality-specific diffuser heads. This architecture enables novel multi-context blending operations that mix style and semantic content from multiple source images or text prompts.

name: TM2T
tokenizer: Motion Tokenizer: VQ-VAE (encodes continuous 3D motion sequences into discrete motion tokens)
task: Text-to-Motion Generation, Motion-to-Text Translation
dataset: Training & Benchmarks: HumanML3D, KIT Motion-Language dataset
metric:
Motion Generation: FID, Diversity, Multimodality (MModality), R-Precision
Motion-Text Alignment: MM-Distance
Text Generation: BLEU@1, BLEU@4, ROUGE, CIDEr, BERTScore
highlight: TM2T jointly addresses text-to-motion generation and motion-to-text translation within a single framework by representing 3D human motions as discrete motion tokens via VQ-VAE, then adapting a neural machine translation model for bidirectional motion-language translation. Discrete motion tokens prevent trivial motionless pose outputs and enable the NMT model to treat motion as a structured sequence language.

name: Unified-IO
tokenizer: Text: SentencePiece; Image: VQ-GAN (encodes images into discrete visual tokens); all outputs decoded via corresponding de-tokenizers
backbone: T5 (encoder-decoder Transformer); sizes from Small (71M) to XL (~2.9B)
task: Image Generation, Image Captioning, Visual Question Answering, Referring Expression Comprehension, Object Detection, Depth Estimation, Image Classification, Surface Normal Estimation, Pose Estimation, Text Grounding, NLP (QA, NLI, Paraphrase)
dataset:
Training: 90+ diverse datasets covering vision, language, and multimodal tasks (custom unified corpus)
Benchmarks: GRIT (7-task benchmark); NYUv2 (depth); ImageNet, Places365 (classification); VQA 2.0, A-OKVQA, VizWiz-QA (VQA); NoCaps, COCO Captions (captioning); RefCOCO (grounding); MRPC, BoolQ, SciTail (NLP)
metric:
Depth: RMSE
Classification: Accuracy
VQA: Accuracy
Captioning: CIDEr
Grounding: Accuracy @ IoU 0.5
NLP: F1 / Accuracy
highlight: Unified-IO is the first model to perform all 7 tasks on the GRIT benchmark simultaneously, covering categorization, localization, VQA, referring expressions, segmentation, keypoints, and surface normals. It achieves this by converting every input and output — images, text, depth maps, bounding boxes — into a sequence of discrete tokens, enabling a single T5-based encoder-decoder to handle over 90 diverse vision and language tasks.

name: DU-VLG
tokenizer: Text: RoBERTa tokenizer (BPE); Image Encoder: ViT; Image Decoder: VQ-VAE (discrete image token generation)
backbone: Custom encoder-decoder (RoBERTa-based text encoder + ViT image encoder + VQ-VAE image decoder)
task: Image Captioning, Text-to-Image Generation, Text-Driven Image Inpainting, Image-Driven Text Infilling, Visual Commonsense Reasoning
dataset:
Pre-training: COCO, Conceptual Captions (CC), SBU Captions, Visual Genome (VG)
Fine-tuning: CUB (text-to-image), COCO (image captioning + text-to-image), VisualCOMET (reasoning)
Benchmarks: COCO (captioning & generation), CUB (generation), VisualCOMET
metric:
Text-to-Image Generation: IS, FID
Image Captioning: BLEU-4, METEOR, CIDEr, SPICE
Visual Commonsense Reasoning: BLEU-2, METEOR, CIDEr
highlight: DU-VLG (Dual Unified Vision-Language Generation) unifies vision-language generation as a sequence generation problem with two novel dual pre-training tasks: a multi-modal denoising autoencoder (masking and reconstructing tokens from either modality) and a modality translation task (bidirectional image-to-text and text-to-image generation). This dual training strategy enables a single model to perform both generation directions in a unified framework.

name: OFA
tokenizer: Shared: custom unified vocabulary (text BPE + visual region coordinates + image patch tokens); Image features via ResNet CNN + object detection tokens
backbone: ResNet (image encoder: ResNet-50 / 101 / 152) + Transformer encoder-decoder (text + multimodal)
task: Visual Grounding, Grounded Captioning, Image-Text Matching, Image Captioning, Visual Question Answering, Object Detection, Image Infilling, Text Infilling
dataset:
Pretraining: custom unified corpus across vision-language and language tasks
Benchmarks: VQAv2, SNLI-VE (VQA/NLI); COCO Captions (captioning); RefCOCO, RefCOCO+, RefCOCOg (grounding); GLUE, Gigaword (NLP); ImageNet-1K (classification)
metric:
Image Captioning: BLEU-4, METEOR, CIDEr, SPICE
VQA: Accuracy
Text-to-Image Generation: FID, CLIP-SIM, IS
Referring Expression: Accuracy @ IoU 0.5
Image Classification: Top-1 Accuracy
NLU: Accuracy / F1
NLG: ROUGE-1/2/L
highlight: OFA (One For All) is a task-agnostic, modality-agnostic sequence-to-sequence framework that unifies diverse cross-modal and unimodal tasks (image generation, captioning, VQA, grounding, NLU/NLG) using a shared vocabulary and a single encoder-decoder backbone. Unlike prior work requiring task-specific architectures or fine-tuning heads, OFA is trained with simple multimodal pretraining objectives and achieves strong results across all tasks without task-specific components.

name: HealthGPT
tokenizer: Visual Encoder: ViT (pre-trained vision transformer); separate H-LoRA adapters per task type
backbone: Large Language Model (LLM) with Heterogeneous Low-Rank Adaptation (H-LoRA) plugin modules; 3.8B and 7B variants
task: Medical Visual Question Answering, Radiology Report Generation, Medical Image-to-Image Synthesis (CT-to-MRI), Medical Image Super-Resolution
dataset:
Training: VL-Health (custom curated dataset with 7 comprehension + 5 generation tasks across medical imaging)
Benchmarks: OmniMedVQA (medical VQA); task-specific medical imaging benchmarks for synthesis
metric:
Medical VQA: Accuracy (74.4% on OmniMedVQA)
Image Synthesis: SSIM (79.38 on CT-to-MRI)
Generation Quality: task-specific metrics
highlight: HealthGPT unifies medical image comprehension and generation in a single model by introducing Heterogeneous Low-Rank Adaptation (H-LoRA), a hierarchical visual perception mechanism that decouples abstract semantic features (for diagnosis/VQA) from fine-grained spatial details (for image synthesis). Task-specific LoRA plugins enable switching between comprehension and generation modes while achieving 67% faster inference than Mixture-of-Experts baselines.

name: Med-PaLM 2
tokenizer: Text: SentencePiece (PaLM 2); Image: ViT patch tokenization
backbone: PaLM 2 (LLM backbone, Unicorn scale) + ViT (image encoder)
parameter: Not disclosed (PaLM 2 scale, ~540B estimated for largest variant)
task: Medical Question Answering, Clinical Reasoning, Radiology Report Summarization, Medical Knowledge Retrieval
dataset:
Training: Medical licensing exam data, biomedical literature, clinical notes (curated)
Benchmarks: MedQA (USMLE), MedMCQA, PubMedQA, MultiMedBench (radiology, pathology, genomics QA)
metric:
Medical QA: Accuracy (USMLE-style), Expert clinician evaluation
Radiology: BLEU, ROUGE, expert accuracy rating
ps: Clinical decision support, medical question answering, automated documentation
highlight: Med-PaLM 2 achieves physician-level performance on medical licensing exam questions (USMLE) and represents the first AI system to cross the expert physician threshold on MedQA benchmarks. It combines PaLM 2's strong reasoning with multimodal medical inputs (imaging, genomics, language) and instruction fine-tuning on curated medical data to produce clinically aligned responses.

name: LLaVA-Med
tokenizer: Image Encoder: CLIP ViT-L/14 (visual feature extraction); Text: LLaMA tokenizer (BPE)
backbone: LLaVA (CLIP ViT-L/14 + Vicuna-13B); fine-tuned with biomedical curriculum learning
task: Biomedical Visual Question Answering, Open-ended Medical Image Instruction Following, Biomedical Figure Understanding
dataset:
Training Stage 1: PubMed Central biomedical figure-caption pairs (large-scale web-crawled)
Training Stage 2: GPT-4 generated instruction-following data from biomedical captions
Benchmarks: VQA-RAD, SLAKE, PathVQA (three standard biomedical VQA datasets)
metric:
Closed-ended VQA: Accuracy
Open-ended VQA: BLEU-1, Recall
Outperforms supervised SOTA on several biomedical VQA benchmarks
ps: Biomedical research, visual question answering, teaching
highlight: LLaVA-Med adapts the general-purpose LLaVA architecture to the biomedical domain via a curriculum learning strategy: Stage 1 aligns vocabulary using biomedical figure-caption pairs, Stage 2 trains conversational ability using GPT-4-generated instruction data from the same captions. The entire training completes in under 15 hours on 8 A100 GPUs, demonstrating efficient domain adaptation of large vision-language models.

name: Stanford FMSM
tokenizer: Structured EHR data tokenizer (tabular clinical events encoded as tokens)
backbone: BERT-style Transformer pre-trained on electronic health records
task: Disease Risk Prediction, Patient Stratification, Clinical Event Forecasting, EHR Representation Learning
dataset:
Training: Multi-center EHR data from Stanford and partner institutions (de-identified)
Benchmarks: Multi-center evaluation on disease prediction tasks
metric: AUROC, Accuracy, Calibration (across disease prediction tasks)
ps: Disease risk prediction, patient stratification, healthcare resource management
highlight: Stanford FMSM is a shared foundation model for electronic health records evaluated in a multi-center study across different hospitals, demonstrating that a single pre-trained EHR model can adapt effectively to diverse clinical settings with minimal site-specific fine-tuning. The study provides evidence for transferability of EHR foundation models across patient populations and institutional data distributions.

name: DriveGPT4
tokenizer: Video Encoder: CLIP-based visual frame encoder; Text: LLM tokenizer
backbone: Multimodal Large Language Model (MLLM) with visual instruction tuning for autonomous driving
task: Interpretable End-to-End Autonomous Driving, Vehicle Control Signal Prediction, Driving Action Interpretation, Driving Scene Question Answering
dataset:
Training: Custom visual instruction tuning dataset tailored for autonomous driving + BDD-X data (mix-finetuning strategy)
Benchmarks: BDD-X dataset; comparison against GPT-4V
metric:
Autonomous Driving: Qualitative and quantitative grounding performance; vehicle control prediction accuracy
QA: Comparison with GPT-4V on driving understanding tasks
ps: Interpretable end-to-end control, visual question answering for driving
highlight: DriveGPT4 is the first interpretable end-to-end autonomous driving system leveraging LLMs, which processes multi-frame video and textual queries to simultaneously predict low-level vehicle control signals and provide natural language explanations for its decisions. A domain-specific visual instruction tuning dataset and mix-finetuning strategy enable performance comparable to GPT-4V on driving understanding tasks.

name: Unified Multimodal LLM Framework
tokenizer: Modality-specific: BLIP/CLIP (images/video), Wav2Vec2 (audio), BERT (time-series/text)
backbone: Hybrid: BERT (time-series), Wav2Vec2 (audio), BLIP/CLIP (visual), T5 (text generation)
task: Traffic Motion Planning, Time-Series Prediction, Audio Classification, Visual Scene Analysis
dataset: Training & Evaluation: Oxford Radar RobotCar, D-Behavior (D-Set), nuScenes, Comma2k19
metric: Average Accuracy: 91.33% across datasets; Time-series: 92.7%; Dataset-specific accuracy per modality
ps: Unified multimodal processing for motion planning and predictive analysis
highlight: This framework consolidates time-series, audio, image, and video modalities into a single unified LLM using modality-specific encoders (BERT, Wav2Vec2, BLIP/CLIP) with a shared T5 decoder, reducing the complexity of deploying multiple specialized models for autonomous driving applications. Evaluated on four real-world autonomous driving datasets, it achieves 91.33% average accuracy across diverse multimodal tasks.

name: LLaVA-ST
tokenizer: Video Encoder: LLaVA-family visual encoder + Language-Aligned Positional Embedding (LAPE) for coordinate tokens; Spatial-Temporal Packer (STP) with dual point-to-region attention streams
backbone: LLaVA-family MLLM with LAPE + Spatial-Temporal Packer modules
task: Spatial-Temporal Video Grounding (STVG), Event Localization and Captioning (ELC), Spatial Video Grounding (SVG)
dataset:
Training: ST-Align (4.3M samples for fine-grained spatial-temporal understanding, custom curated with progressive coarse-to-fine pipeline)
Benchmarks: 11 spatial-temporal understanding benchmarks; ST-Align benchmark (introduced by authors)
metric:
Spatial-Temporal Grounding: IoU, mAP
Event Localization: R@K, mIoU
Spatial Grounding: Accuracy @ IoU threshold
ps: Fine-grained spatial-temporal multimodal understanding, precise localization and description
highlight: LLaVA-ST addresses the challenge of simultaneous temporal and spatial localization in video understanding through two novel modules: Language-Aligned Positional Embedding (LAPE) that embeds textual coordinate tokens into the visual space, and the Spatial-Temporal Packer (STP) that decouples temporal and spatial feature compression into separate attention streams. A 4.3M-sample ST-Align dataset with a coarse-to-fine training curriculum enables state-of-the-art performance across 11 spatial-temporal benchmarks.

name: SeeUnsafe
tokenizer: Off-the-shelf MLLM encoders (e.g., GPT-4V, LLaVA visual encoders)
backbone: Off-the-shelf MLLMs (GPT-4V, LLaVA) used as backbone agents
task: Traffic Video Accident Classification, Visual Grounding of Accident Events, Interactive Traffic Safety Analysis
dataset:
Evaluation: Toyota Woven Traffic Safety dataset
Benchmarks: Accident-aware video classification; visual grounding evaluation
metric: Information Matching Score (IMS, novel MLLM-based metric for structured response alignment), Accident Classification Accuracy, Visual Grounding Performance
highlight: SeeUnsafe introduces a conversational MLLM-based framework for traffic accident analysis that transforms the traditional extract-then-explain workflow into an interactive session with severity-aware video aggregation and novel multimodal prompts for fine-grained visual grounding. A new Information Matching Score (IMS) metric using MLLM evaluation enables structured comparison of generated accident reports against ground truth.

name: AccidentGPT
tokenizer: Modality-specific encoders for video, images, and sensor data; central LLM for reasoning
backbone: Hybrid: Modality-specific encoders + Central LLM (GPT-style) for integrated reasoning
task: Traffic Accident Reconstruction, Accident Causality Analysis, Safety Warning Generation, 3D BEV Scene Understanding, Liability Determination, Insurance Report Generation
dataset: Training & Evaluation: Multi-modal traffic datasets with V2X communication data, dashcam footage, sensor logs
metric: Task-dependent: Reconstruction Accuracy, Classification Performance, Report Quality
ps: Comprehensive traffic accident analysis and reconstruction. Note: paper arXiv ID may be incorrect in source data.
highlight: AccidentGPT is a foundational model for traffic safety that unifies V2X (vehicle-to-everything) environmental perception, collaborative multi-vehicle sensing, and LLM-based reasoning for comprehensive accident analysis. The system jointly processes multi-camera video, sensor data, and GPS/IMU signals to perform accident reconstruction, causality analysis, and generate structured reports and insurance claims.

name: SurrealDriver
tokenizer: LLM tokenizer (text-based scene descriptions and driving actions)
backbone: Large Language Model (LLM) generative agent with chain-of-thought reasoning
task: Autonomous Urban Driving Decision-Making, Human-Like Driving Behavior Generation, Collision Avoidance
dataset:
Training: Driving-Thinking Dataset (custom, collected from urban driving experiments with human drivers' verbalized thoughts under diverse conditions)
Evaluation: Simulated driving scenarios + human assessments
metric:
Collision Rate Reduction: 81.04% vs baseline LLM agent
Human Likeness Improvement: 50% vs baseline LLM agent
highlight: SurrealDriver constructs human-like autonomous driving agents by incorporating post-driving self-report driving-thinking data from human operators as both chain-of-thought prompts and demonstration examples for LLM reasoning. By bridging the embodiment gap between LLMs' language reasoning and physical driving decisions using verbalized human cognition, the framework achieves 81.04% collision rate reduction and 50% human-likeness improvement over baseline LLM driving agents.

name: Trafformer
tokenizer: Graph-structured traffic data (node features at each timestamp encoded as input tokens)
backbone: Custom Transformer-style architecture with unified spatio-temporal self-attention matrix and generative decoder
task: Traffic Flow Prediction, Spatio-Temporal Traffic Forecasting
dataset:
Training & Evaluation: Standard traffic prediction datasets (METR-LA, PEMS-BAY or similar benchmark traffic datasets)
Benchmarks: Two standard traffic forecasting benchmark datasets
metric: MAE (Mean Absolute Error), RMSE, MAPE (standard traffic prediction metrics)
ps: Unified spatio-temporal dependency modeling for traffic flow prediction
highlight: Trafformer unifies spatial and temporal dependency modeling in a single self-attention operation, allowing every node at every timestamp to attend to every other node at every other timestamp in one forward pass — eliminating the iterative recurrence of prior methods. A generative decoder enables multi-step prediction in a single forward operation, improving both accuracy and computational efficiency for traffic forecasting.

name: PLIP
tokenizer: Image Encoder: ViT-B/32 (CLIP image encoder fine-tuned for pathology); Text Encoder: Transformer text encoder (CLIP text backbone)
backbone: CLIP (ViT-B/32 + text transformer), fine-tuned from OpenAI CLIP on pathology image-text pairs
task: Pathology Image Classification (Zero-shot), Cancer Subtype Recognition, Tissue Type Classification, Bidirectional Image-Text Retrieval for Pathology
dataset:
Training: OpenPath (208,414 pathology image-text pairs from medical Twitter posts and LAION-5B)
Benchmarks: Kather Colon, PanNuke, and two additional pathology classification datasets (4 total)
metric:
Zero-shot Classification: F1 Score (0.565–0.832 vs CLIP 0.030–0.481)
Supervised Fine-tuning: F1 Score (+2.5% over CLIP)
Retrieval: Recall@K
ps: Computational pathology, zero-shot image classification, oncology research
highlight: PLIP (Pathology Language-Image Pretraining) is a specialized CLIP foundation model for computational pathology, trained on the OpenPath dataset of 208,414 clinician-annotated pathology image-text pairs collected from medical Twitter. PLIP achieves dramatically higher zero-shot F1 scores (0.565–0.832) compared to generic CLIP (0.030–0.481) on pathology classification benchmarks, enabling zero-shot diagnosis and bidirectional image-text retrieval for clinical and educational applications.

name: DiffusionDrive
tokenizer: Multi-view image encoder (BEVFormer with ResNet-50 backbone for bird's-eye-view feature extraction); vectorized scene representation
task: End-to-End Autonomous Driving Planning, Vectorized Scene Understanding (agents, maps, ego-motion)
dataset:
Training & Evaluation: nuScenes
Benchmarks: nuScenes open-loop planning evaluation
metric: Collision Rate (L2 distance error), Inference Speed (FPS), Planning L2 error
ps: Fully vectorized scene representation, avoids rasterized map representation
highlight: This work (VAD) proposes a fully vectorized paradigm for end-to-end autonomous driving that represents all driving scene elements (agents, maps, ego trajectory) as vector entities rather than dense rasterized grids, achieving a 29% reduction in collision rate and 2.5× faster inference than prior methods. Vectorized scene representation enables explicit interaction modeling between the ego vehicle and all environment elements through structured attention.

name: Unified World Models (UWM)
tokenizer: Multimodal Diffusion Transformer tokenizer for video frames and action sequences
backbone: Multimodal Diffusion Transformer with independent modality-specific diffusion timesteps
task: Robot Policy Learning (Imitation Learning), Forward Dynamics Prediction, Inverse Dynamics Prediction, Video Generation
dataset:
Training: Large-scale multitask robot datasets + action-free video datasets (heterogeneous, including datasets lacking complete action annotations)
Benchmarks: Simulated and real-world robotic manipulation tasks
metric: Policy Success Rate, Generalization across tasks, Robustness vs imitation learning baselines
ps: Learns unified world dynamics model for policy learning and video prediction
highlight: Unified World Models (UWM) couples video and action diffusion within a single transformer by assigning independent diffusion timesteps to each modality, enabling the model to be trained on heterogeneous datasets that may lack complete action or video annotations. This unified pre-training naturally learns imitation learning, forward/inverse dynamics, and video generation simultaneously, improving generalization of robotic control policies.

name: VLAD
tokenizer: Multi-camera image encoder (VLM-based visual encoder); text encoder for contextual commands
task: Autonomous Vehicle Planning, Semantic Scene Understanding, Hierarchical Motion Planning, Interpretable Decision Generation
dataset:
Training & Evaluation: Standard autonomous driving benchmarks
Benchmarks: Autonomous driving planning evaluation (performance and interpretability metrics)
metric: Planning Performance: standard autonomous driving metrics (collision rate, route completion); Interpretability: decision explanation quality
highlight: VLAD (VLM-Augmented Autonomous Driving) integrates Vision-Language Models into a hierarchical autonomous driving planning framework, using VLM semantic understanding to enhance high-level route planning while maintaining interpretability of each driving decision. The hierarchical architecture separates semantic reasoning from low-level trajectory generation, enabling transparent and explainable autonomous driving behavior.
